text:
RAHUL VENKATARAMAN

Sr. Data Engineer

LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/rahul-venkataraman | Phone: +1
(469)-532-7814 | rahul.venkataraman@gmail.com | Dallas, TX

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

-   10+ years of experience as a Data Engineer designing, building, and
    optimizing large-scale data pipelines, ETL frameworks, and
    cloud-native data platforms across healthcare, financial services,
    retail, telecom, energy, and e-commerce domains.

-   Designed and deployed production-grade data orchestration pipelines
    using Apache Airflow, Dagster, and AWS Glue on AWS, leveraging ECS,
    EventBridge, Lambda, and S3 to ingest, transform, and deliver
    enterprise datasets reliably at scale.

-   Built real-time streaming architectures using Apache Kafka, AWS
    Kinesis Data Streams, and Spark Streaming, enabling sub-second event
    processing for fraud detection, IoT telemetry, and customer
    analytics platforms.

-   Expert in Apache Spark (PySpark/Scala) for large-scale distributed
    data processing, implementing advanced optimizations including
    partition tuning, broadcast joins, caching, and dynamic resource
    allocation on AWS EMR and Databricks.

-   Proficient in cloud data warehousing with Amazon Redshift,
    Snowflake, Google BigQuery, and Azure Synapse, designing
    star/snowflake schemas, optimizing distribution keys, and
    implementing data vault modeling for enterprise analytics.

-   Strong expertise in AWS data services including Glue, EMR, Kinesis,
    Lake Formation, Athena, S3, Lambda, Step Functions, DMS, and
    DataSync for end-to-end data lake and lakehouse architectures.

-   Implemented data quality frameworks using Great Expectations, AWS
    Deequ, and custom Python validation libraries, enforcing schema
    validation, null checks, referential integrity, and anomaly
    detection across production data pipelines.

-   Experienced with dbt (data build tool) for SQL-based transformation
    layers, implementing modular data models, incremental strategies,
    source freshness checks, and documentation for analytics engineering
    workflows.

-   Built observability and monitoring frameworks using AWS CloudWatch,
    Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, and Collibra, implementing metrics
    collection, distributed tracing, data lineage tracking, and alerting
    across data platforms.

-   Hands-on experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies, leading sprint
    planning, architecture reviews, and mentoring junior data engineers
    across geographically distributed teams.

TECHNICAL SKILLS

Programming Languages: Python, PySpark, Scala, SQL, Bash, Java, HiveQL,
R

Big Data & Processing: Apache Spark, PySpark, Spark Streaming, Apache
Kafka, Apache Flink, Apache Hive, HDFS, MapReduce, Yarn, Apache NiFi,
Sqoop, Flume, Zookeeper

Cloud – AWS: S3, Glue, EMR, Lambda, ECS, EKS, Kinesis (Streams &
Firehose), Redshift, Athena, Lake Formation, DMS, DataSync, Step
Functions, EventBridge, SQS, SNS, CloudWatch, CDK, CloudFormation, IAM,
VPC, Secrets Manager, SageMaker

Cloud – Azure & GCP: Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure
Synapse, Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2, Azure Event Hubs, Azure SQL,
CosmosDB, GCP BigQuery, GCP Dataproc, GCP Pub/Sub, GCP Cloud Storage,
GCP Dataflow

Data Warehousing & BI: Amazon Redshift, Snowflake, Google BigQuery,
Azure Synapse, Teradata, Star/Snowflake Schema, Data Vault, Tableau,
Power BI, Amazon QuickSight, Looker, Splunk

Databases: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle 12c/11g, MS SQL Server, MongoDB,
Cassandra, DynamoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, HBase

ETL & Orchestration: AWS Glue, Apache Airflow, Dagster, dbt, Informatica
PowerCenter 10.x, Talend 6.x, SSIS, AWS Step Functions, Prefect

Data Quality & Governance: Great Expectations, AWS Deequ, Apache Atlas,
Collibra, Alation, ERWIN, AWS Lake Formation

DevOps & CI/CD: Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, AWS CDK,
Git, GitHub, GitLab, SonarQube, Maven, Gradle

Monitoring & Observability: AWS CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana,
Datadog, Splunk, ELK Stack, PagerDuty, AWS X-Ray

ML & AI Integration: AWS SageMaker, MLflow, DSPy, LangChain, NumPy,
Pandas, Scikit-Learn, MLLIB

Methodologies: Agile/Scrum, SAFe, Data Mesh, Data Lakehouse,
Lambda/Kappa Architecture, TDD, SDLC

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

UnitedHealth Group (Client: Optum Analytics): October 2023 – Current
(Location: Eden Prairie, MN (Remote))

Role: Sr. Data Engineer

Responsibilities:

-   Designed and deployed HIPAA-compliant AWS Glue and Apache Spark ETL
    pipelines ingesting 50M+ daily healthcare claims, member eligibility
    records, and clinical encounter data from HL7 FHIR APIs and EDI
    837/835 feeds into an AWS S3 data lake partitioned by payer, date,
    and claim type.

-   Built real-time streaming pipelines using AWS Kinesis Data Streams
    and Apache Kafka, processing prior authorization events and pharmacy
    benefit transactions with sub-500ms latency, triggering downstream
    Lambda enrichment functions and Redshift Streaming Ingestion for
    live dashboards.

-   Implemented dbt transformation models on Amazon Redshift building a
    semantic layer for HEDIS quality measures, risk adjustment factor
    (RAF) scoring, and population health KPIs, enabling self-service
    analytics for 200+ clinical analysts via Tableau and QuickSight.

-   Orchestrated end-to-end pipeline workflows using Apache Airflow on
    AWS MWAA, managing DAG dependencies for claims adjudication,
    provider network updates, and member enrollment backfills,
    implementing SLA alerts and retry policies with CloudWatch and
    PagerDuty integration.

-   Built a AWS Lake Formation governed data lakehouse with fine-grained
    column-level access controls for PHI data, implementing AWS Glue
    Data Catalog, row-level security policies, and HIPAA-compliant audit
    trails via CloudTrail and Collibra lineage tracking.

-   Developed Great Expectations data quality suites validating
    referential integrity between member IDs, provider NPIs, and claim
    line items, embedding checkpoint validations in Airflow DAGs and
    publishing HTML quality reports to S3 for compliance review.

-   Engineered AWS CDK infrastructure-as-code provisioning EMR clusters,
    Glue job environments, Kinesis shards, Redshift clusters, and Lake
    Formation permissions, enabling repeatable environment deployments
    with HIPAA/HITECH compliance guardrails.

-   Integrated AWS SageMaker ML pipelines with data engineering
    workflows, building feature stores and training dataset preparation
    pipelines in PySpark for readmission risk, chronic disease
    progression, and pharmacy adherence prediction models.

-   Implemented Collibra data governance workflows for PHI
    classification, data stewardship, business glossary management, and
    automated impact analysis across 300+ datasets in the enterprise
    data lake, ensuring HIPAA, HITECH, and CMS compliance.

-   Built AWS Step Functions state machines orchestrating multi-step ETL
    workflows for CMS regulatory reporting (HEDIS, STARS, ACA MLR),
    coordinating Glue jobs, Lambda validators, Redshift loads, and SNS
    notifications with error compensation logic.

-   Optimized PySpark jobs on AWS EMR processing historical claims
    backfills of 5+ billion records, implementing adaptive query
    execution, dynamic partition pruning, and columnar Parquet storage
    reducing job runtime from 14 hours to 2.5 hours.

Environment: Python, PySpark, Apache Spark, AWS Glue, AWS EMR, AWS
Kinesis, Apache Kafka, Amazon Redshift, dbt, Apache Airflow (MWAA), AWS
Lake Formation, AWS Step Functions, AWS CDK, Great Expectations,
Collibra, AWS SageMaker, MLflow, Tableau, Power BI, CloudWatch,
PagerDuty, Terraform

JPMorgan Chase & Co.: June 2021 – September 2023 (Location: Columbus,
OH)

Role: Sr. Data Engineer

Responsibilities:

-   Built PCI-DSS and SOX-compliant Apache Kafka event streaming
    pipelines ingesting real-time payment card transactions, wire
    transfers, and ACH events from 40+ source systems, processing 8M+
    daily events into S3 data lake and Redshift for fraud analytics and
    regulatory capital reporting.

-   Designed Snowflake data warehouse on AWS with multi-cluster
    configurations, implementing data vault 2.0 modeling for customer
    360, account hierarchy, and transaction history, creating role-based
    access controls for 1,500+ analytics consumers across risk,
    compliance, and business intelligence teams.

-   Developed PySpark Spark Structured Streaming jobs on AWS EMR for
    real-time fraud scoring, joining card transaction streams with
    merchant risk profiles and behavioral models from DynamoDB,
    triggering Lambda-based case creation in the fraud operations
    platform within 150ms.

-   Implemented AWS DMS and custom Python CDC pipelines migrating 200TB
    of Oracle and DB2 mainframe banking data to S3 data lake, designing
    incremental extraction strategies using log-based CDC, watermark
    columns, and Glue bookmarks for zero-downtime migration.

-   Built dbt projects on Snowflake modeling regulatory datasets for
    Basel III RWA calculation, CCAR stress testing, and FRTB market
    risk, implementing incremental materialization strategies and
    automated documentation with dbt docs deployed on S3.

-   Orchestrated complex financial data workflows using Dagster on AWS
    ECS, managing asset-based pipelines for trade settlement, position
    reconciliation, and P&L reporting, implementing sensor-based
    triggers for real-time market data events from Bloomberg and Reuters
    feeds.

-   Engineered AWS Glue DataBrew transformation recipes standardizing
    customer PII data, applying tokenization, format normalization, and
    de-duplication across 80+ source feeds from retail banking,
    investment banking, and credit card platforms under GLBA compliance.

-   Implemented Apache Atlas and AWS Glue Data Catalog for enterprise
    data lineage, classifying 500+ datasets with business metadata, PII
    tags, and retention policies, enabling automated data discovery and
    impact analysis for regulatory audit readiness.

-   Built AWS Lambda and EventBridge-driven micro-batch pipelines for
    intraday position updates and trade confirmations, processing FIX
    protocol messages from OMS systems and loading enriched trade
    records into Redshift and DynamoDB with SOX-compliant audit logging.

-   Developed Terraform and AWS CDK modules standardizing data platform
    infrastructure provisioning across 12 data engineering teams,
    including EMR cluster templates, Glue job configurations, Kinesis
    shard calculators, and Redshift WLM policies.

Environment: Python, PySpark, Apache Spark, Apache Kafka, AWS Glue, AWS
EMR, AWS DMS, Amazon Redshift, Snowflake, dbt, Dagster, AWS Lambda, AWS
EventBridge, DynamoDB, Apache Atlas, AWS CDK, Terraform, Jenkins,
Docker, Kubernetes, CloudWatch, Datadog, Power BI

Walmart Global Tech (Client: Sam's Club): August 2019 – May 2021
(Location: Bentonville, AR)

Role: Data Engineer

Responsibilities:

-   Designed Apache Spark batch and streaming data pipelines on AWS EMR
    processing 100TB+ daily retail transaction data from 600+ Sam's Club
    locations, ingesting POS sales, inventory movements, membership
    events, and supplier feeds into a centralized S3 data lake with
    Delta Lake ACID transaction support.

-   Built Apache Kafka Streams and AWS Kinesis Firehose pipelines for
    real-time inventory visibility, processing item scan events, RFID
    tags, and warehouse management system updates, enabling same-day
    inventory accuracy improvements from 78% to 97% across distribution
    centers.

-   Developed PySpark ETL jobs on AWS Glue transforming supplier EDI
    856/810 feeds, product catalog updates, and demand signals into
    curated data marts in Redshift, powering QuickSight dashboards for
    category managers, supply chain planners, and merchant teams.

-   Implemented Apache Airflow DAGs on Amazon MWAA orchestrating
    end-to-end merchandise planning workflows, coordinating Spark jobs,
    Glue crawlers, Redshift COPY commands, and dbt model runs with SLA
    monitoring and automatic failure alerting via SNS and Slack.

-   Built a self-service data lakehouse using AWS Lake Formation and
    Athena, enabling 300+ business analysts to query curated sales,
    membership, and inventory datasets using SQL without infrastructure
    management, reducing ad-hoc data request turnaround from 5 days to 2
    hours.

-   Designed dbt models on Redshift building member lifetime value,
    basket analysis, and item affinity datasets for the personalization
    platform, implementing incremental models with snapshot strategies
    for slowly changing member demographics and purchase history.

-   Integrated AWS SageMaker feature stores with data engineering
    pipelines, building automated feature pipelines extracting member
    behavioral signals from clickstream data (AWS Kinesis + EMR) and
    transactional history for product recommendation ML models.

-   Implemented Great Expectations checkpoints in Airflow DAGs
    validating daily sales, inventory, and membership datasets,
    publishing data quality scorecards to Confluence and triggering
    PagerDuty alerts for SLA-breaching quality failures before data
    reaches downstream consumers.

-   Built AWS CDK data platform templates standardizing EMR cluster
    provisioning, S3 bucket lifecycle policies, Lake Formation
    permissions, and Glue job configurations across 8 data engineering
    squads, reducing new pipeline setup time from 3 weeks to 2 days.

Environment: Python, PySpark, Apache Spark, Apache Kafka, AWS Glue, AWS
EMR, Amazon MWAA, AWS Kinesis, Delta Lake, Amazon Redshift, dbt, AWS
Lake Formation, Amazon Athena, AWS SageMaker, Great Expectations, AWS
CDK, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, QuickSight, CloudWatch, Grafana

AT&T Inc.: March 2017 – July 2019 (Location: Dallas, TX)

Role: Data Engineer

Responsibilities:

-   Built Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming pipelines ingesting 2B+ daily
    network CDR (Call Detail Records), data usage events, and IoT device
    telemetry from 5G and LTE network probes into AWS S3, enabling
    real-time network quality analytics and customer experience
    monitoring for 230M+ subscribers.

-   Developed PySpark data transformation jobs on AWS EMR, processing
    subscriber churn prediction features, network congestion signals,
    and revenue assurance datasets for the enterprise analytics
    platform, optimizing job performance with dynamic allocation and
    partition coalescing.

-   Implemented AWS Glue crawler and Data Catalog automating schema
    discovery across 400+ S3 data sources including network logs,
    billing records, and CRM exports, enabling Athena-based ad-hoc
    querying and Lake Formation governed access for network operations
    and marketing analytics teams.

-   Built Teradata to Amazon Redshift migration pipelines using AWS DMS
    and custom Python extraction scripts, migrating 150TB of subscriber
    billing, plan, and usage data with table re-partitioning, sort key
    optimization, and WLM queue configuration for query performance
    parity.

-   Designed Apache Airflow DAGs orchestrating telecom regulatory
    reporting workflows (FCC, CPUC), coordinating monthly CDR
    aggregation, quality validation, and encrypted file delivery to
    regulatory agencies with audit trail logging in S3 and Redshift.

-   Developed Python and Scala Spark jobs for subscriber segmentation,
    usage pattern analysis, and ARPU (Average Revenue Per User)
    modeling, loading results into Snowflake for consumption by CRM,
    marketing automation, and product pricing teams via Tableau
    dashboards.

-   Implemented AWS Lambda and SQS-based event processing for real-time
    provisioning of network services, processing order management events
    and triggering network configuration APIs for 5G service activation,
    eSIM provisioning, and device upgrade workflows.

-   Built data quality monitoring using AWS Deequ on Spark, validating
    subscriber PII completeness, CDR record counts, and billing cycle
    integrity across 300+ daily data feeds, publishing metrics to
    CloudWatch and triggering automated data incident tickets in
    ServiceNow.

Environment: Python, PySpark, Scala, Apache Kafka, Apache Spark, AWS
Glue, AWS EMR, AWS DMS, Amazon Redshift, Snowflake, Apache Airflow, AWS
Kinesis, Amazon Athena, AWS Lambda, AWS SQS, AWS Deequ, AWS CDK,
Terraform, Jenkins, Docker, Tableau, CloudWatch, Splunk

ExxonMobil Corporation: June 2015 – February 2017 (Location: Houston,
TX)

Role: Data Engineer / ETL Developer

Responsibilities:

-   Built Apache Spark and Hadoop MapReduce pipelines on AWS EMR
    processing seismic survey data, well production logs, and refinery
    sensor telemetry from 500+ upstream and downstream assets, ingesting
    20TB+ daily operational data into S3 for reservoir modeling and
    production optimization analytics.

-   Developed Informatica PowerCenter ETL workflows integrating SAP
    PM/ERP, OSIsoft PI historian, and SCADA systems for equipment
    maintenance, production KPIs, and energy consumption data, loading
    into Teradata enterprise data warehouse for HSE reporting and
    executive dashboards.

-   Designed AWS Glue Python Shell and PySpark jobs migrating legacy
    Informatica pipelines to cloud-native ETL on S3, transforming
    drilling operations, completion costs, and hydrocarbon production
    data with schema evolution support and backfill automation.

-   Implemented Apache Airflow orchestration for daily production
    reporting workflows, coordinating data extraction from field
    historian systems, Spark transformation jobs, Redshift loads, and
    automated Power BI dataset refreshes for upstream production teams.

-   Built real-time IoT sensor data pipelines using AWS Kinesis Data
    Streams and Lambda, ingesting pressure, temperature, and flow rate
    readings from 10,000+ wellhead sensors, applying anomaly detection
    algorithms to trigger automated alerts for equipment failure
    prevention.

-   Developed Python-based data quality frameworks validating production
    volume reconciliation between field measurement systems and
    accounting records, implementing automated variance reporting and
    exception workflows integrated with SAP and the Redshift analytics
    layer.

-   Designed Snowflake data warehouse schemas for upstream exploration
    and downstream refining analytics, implementing multi-dimensional
    models for crude oil pricing, feedstock optimization, and margin
    analysis, integrating with Bloomberg commodity data feeds via AWS
    Lambda.

Environment: Python, PySpark, Apache Spark, Hadoop, AWS Glue, AWS EMR,
AWS Kinesis, Amazon Redshift, Snowflake, Teradata, Informatica
PowerCenter, Apache Airflow, AWS Lambda, AWS S3, Power BI, Tableau,
Grafana, CloudWatch, Jenkins, Docker

Infosys Limited (Client: Amazon Retail): August 2013 – May 2015
(Location: Seattle, WA)

Role: ETL / Data Engineer

Responsibilities:

-   Built Python and Java ETL pipelines integrating Amazon marketplace
    seller data, inventory feeds, and product catalog updates from 50+
    global fulfillment centers into Amazon Redshift, enabling seller
    performance analytics and inventory forecasting for the Amazon
    Seller Central platform.

-   Developed Apache Hive and Pig scripts on Hadoop processing
    clickstream logs, search query data, and customer review datasets
    (10TB+ daily) for product recommendation and search relevance
    improvement initiatives on Amazon.com.

-   Implemented AWS Glue and S3-based data lake foundations, designing
    S3 partition strategies, Glue Data Catalog schemas, and Athena query
    optimizations for retail analytics, A/B testing result datasets, and
    customer behavioral cohort analysis.

-   Built Talend ETL jobs integrating third-party logistics providers,
    customs data, and import/export compliance records into the supply
    chain analytics platform, automating daily data reconciliation and
    exception reporting workflows.

-   Developed Apache Sqoop and custom JDBC extraction scripts migrating
    Oracle ERP and MySQL operational databases to S3 data lake,
    implementing incremental extraction strategies for order management,
    returns processing, and vendor payment datasets.

-   Implemented Apache Oozie and later Apache Airflow DAGs orchestrating
    Hive, Pig, and Spark batch jobs for nightly sales reporting,
    customer segmentation updates, and inventory position refreshes,
    managing job dependencies across 200+ workflow tasks.

-   Built data quality validation scripts in Python enforcing
    referential integrity between order, customer, and product datasets,
    generating automated data quality scorecards and escalating failures
    to data stewardship teams via SNS and JIRA integration.

Environment: Python, Java, Apache Hive, Apache Pig, Apache Spark, Hadoop
HDFS, Apache Sqoop, Apache Oozie, Apache Airflow, AWS Glue, Amazon S3,
Amazon Redshift, Amazon Athena, Talend, Oracle, MySQL, AWS Lambda,
Jenkins, Git, Power BI, Tableau

CERTIFICATIONS

AWS Certified Data Engineer – Associate – Amazon Web Services (AWS) |
Issued: January 2024 | Credential ID: AWS-DEA-2024-RV9921

AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate – Amazon Web Services
(AWS) | Issued: March 2022 | Credential ID: AWS-SAA-2022-RV7734

AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner – Amazon Web Services (AWS) | Issued:
September 2020 | Credential ID: AWS-CCP-2020-RV3312

Databricks Certified Associate Developer for Apache Spark 3.0 –
Databricks | Issued: July 2023 | Credential ID: DBR-SPARK3-2023-RV0041

Databricks Certified Data Engineer Associate – Databricks | Issued:
November 2023 | Credential ID: DBR-DEA-2023-RV0088

Snowflake SnowPro Core Certification – Snowflake Inc. | Issued: April
2022 | Credential ID: SNOW-CORE-2022-RV5521

Google Professional Data Engineer – Google Cloud (GCP) | Issued: August
2021 | Credential ID: GCP-PDE-2021-RV6644

Confluent Certified Developer for Apache Kafka (CCDAK) – Confluent Inc.
| Issued: February 2023 | Credential ID: CCDAK-2023-CF-RV7712

HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate – HashiCorp | Issued: October
2022 | Credential ID: HCTA-2022-RV-00456

dbt Analytics Engineering Certification – dbt Labs | Issued: June 2023 |
Credential ID: DBT-AEC-2023-RV2209

Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate (DP-203) – Microsoft
| Issued: December 2021 | Credential ID: DP203-2021-MS-RV8812

Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) – Cloud Native
Computing Foundation (CNCF) | Issued: May 2022 | Credential ID:
CKAD-2022-RV-00789

EDUCATION

Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science & Engineering – Osmania
University, Hyderabad, India, 2009–2013

MOUNIKA K
BIG DATA ENGINEER

LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/mounikak30  |  Phone: +1 (860) 375-4345  |  Email: mounikachoudary30@gmail.com


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

- Around 11+ years of professional experience in information technology as a result-driven Big Data
  Engineer with expertise in architecting, implementing, and optimizing data solutions across diverse
  industries.

- Built progressive experience as a Data Engineer, designing and owning end-to-end ETL and ELT
  pipelines using Palantir Foundry, PySpark, Python, and SQL, consistently delivering analytics-ready
  datasets that empowered finance, operations, and revenue teams with reliable, scalable, and governed
  data platforms.

- Developed the blueprint for scalable medallion architectures across cloud ecosystems, structuring
  bronze, silver, and gold layers using PySpark and SQL, ensuring data lineage, validation, and
  traceability while aligning governance frameworks with enterprise security and compliance standards.

- Crafted resilient ingestion frameworks integrating ERP, CRM, POS, and marketing platforms including
  Meta Ads and Google Ads, harmonizing structured and semi-structured datasets through pandas and
  Pipeline Builder workflows to support forecasting, anomaly detection, and executive reporting.

- Architected high-performance transformation pipelines leveraging distributed processing in PySpark,
  optimizing joins, partitioning, and caching strategies to handle large-scale datasets efficiently while
  reducing latency and improving availability within cloud-based SaaS environments.

- Established data governance standards by implementing role-based access controls, metadata
  documentation, and naming conventions within Palantir Foundry, collaborating closely with IT security
  teams to safeguard sensitive financial and healthcare information.

- Devised automated data validation and monitoring workflows, embedding quality checks, schema
  enforcement, and reconciliation logic into ETL pipelines to proactively identify inconsistencies and
  perform root-cause analysis before downstream analytics were impacted.

- Partnered with analytics consumers and business leaders to translate operational questions into curated
  data products, building reusable data models and feature-ready datasets that enabled self-service
  dashboards and trend analysis.

- Constructed custom analytics applications in Foundry Workshop, combining backend transformations in
  PySpark with interactive front-end logic to streamline reporting, automate workflows, and reduce manual
  spreadsheet-based processes.

- Introduced Git-based version control and collaborative development standards, enforcing code reviews,
  branching strategies, and documentation best practices to improve maintainability and reproducibility.

- Managed cloud-native data lake and warehouse ecosystems across AWS, Azure, and GCP, fine-tuning
  storage strategies, optimizing compute utilization, and ensuring resilient architecture aligned with
  enterprise scalability requirements.

- Applied advanced SQL techniques including window functions, CTEs, and performance tuning to shape
  analytics-ready views supporting revenue management, operational efficiency tracking, and executive
  scorecards.

- Mentored junior and mid-level engineers through structured feedback sessions, architecture
  walkthroughs, and modeling reviews, reinforcing expectations around clarity, reusability, and data
  platform ownership.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

ETL                      : Informatica Power Center 10.x/9.6/9.1, AWS Glue, Talend 5.6, SSIS,
                           EHR, Semarchy xDM, Ab Initio
Databases & Tools        : MS SQL Server 2014/2012/2008, Teradata 15/14, Oracle 11g/10g,
                           SQL Assistant, Erwin 8/9, ER Studio, pgAdmin 4, MySQL
Cloud Environment        : AWS (Snowflake, RDS, Aurora, Redshift, EC2, EMR, S3, Lambda, Glue,
                           Data Pipeline, Athena, DMS, SQS, SNS, ELB, VPC, EBS, Route53,
                           CloudWatch, Auto Scaling, Git, CLI, Jenkins)
                           Azure (VMs, HDInsight, CosmosDB, Fabric, Data Warehouse, Blob
                           Storage, Data Lake, Data Factory, Functions, SQL, Databricks)
                           GCP (GCE, DataProc, Firestore, BigQuery, Cloud Storage, GKE,
                           Functions, Spanner, Pub/Sub)
Reporting Tools          : Tableau, Power BI, EHR, Semarchy
Big Data Ecosystem       : HDFS, Hive, MapReduce, Pig, Sqoop, Flume, Oozie, HBase, Spark,
                           Spark Streaming, NiFi, Yarn, Zookeeper, Storm, Airflow, Kafka
Programming Languages    : Unix, SQL, PL/SQL, Perl, Python, T-SQL, Java, Django, PySpark,
                           Spring Boot, Scala
Methodologies            : SDLC, Waterfall, Agile
Containerization         : Kubernetes, Docker, Jenkins, Docker Hub, Docker Swarm
Python Libraries / ML    : Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Scikit-Learn, Decision
                           Tree, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Gradient Boosting, SVM, KNN
Data Warehousing & BI    : Star and Snowflake Schema, Facts and Dimensions, SSRS, SSAS,
                           SSIS, Splunk, Cloudera
Analytics Platforms      : Dataiku DSS, Alteryx
Enterprise Applications  : Dynamics 365 (Finance & Operations / CRM), ServiceNow, Jira, Confluence
Certifications           : Azure AZ-900, Azure DP-203, Tableau Certified, ServiceNow CAD and
                           SaaS Analyst


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

BANK OF AMERICA                                                               Charlotte, NC
Sr. Data Engineer                                                       June 2023 - Current

- Led detailed requirement sessions with finance, risk, and revenue stakeholders to translate regulatory
  reporting mandates into scalable ETL solutions using Palantir Foundry, integrating transactional, ERP,
  and CRM datasets into a governed AWS data lake architecture.

- Designed medallion architecture on Amazon S3, structuring bronze, silver, and gold layers through
  PySpark transformations and advanced SQL modeling, ensuring reconciliation with banking ledgers and
  producing audit-ready datasets for regulatory financial reporting.

- Built ingestion pipelines in Pipeline Builder, integrating loan systems, payment gateways, and Meta
  Ads API feeds, applying schema standardization and validation controls to maintain compliance and
  consistent downstream analytics accuracy.

- Engineered distributed data transformations using PySpark on AWS EMR, optimizing partitioning and
  executor configurations to process high-volume settlement data reliably during peak transaction cycles.

- Implemented role-based access controls within Palantir Foundry and aligned permissions with AWS IAM
  policies, ensuring secure, least-privilege access for analysts, auditors, and operational stakeholders
  handling sensitive financial datasets.

- Developed curated gold datasets powering liquidity forecasting dashboards, leveraging SQL and Python
  to deliver analytics-ready data products that reduced dependency on manual spreadsheet reconciliations.

- Configured monitoring and alerting frameworks using Amazon CloudWatch, embedding data quality checks
  and anomaly detection logic to identify reconciliation discrepancies before executive reporting cycles.

- Optimized complex reporting queries in Amazon Redshift, utilizing window functions, distribution
  styles, and indexing strategies to improve performance during month-end financial consolidation.

- Created interactive analytics tools in Foundry Workshop, combining backend transformations with
  user-driven controls to allow finance teams to evaluate revenue scenarios and operational KPIs.

- Automated reconciliation routines using scheduled AWS Lambda functions and parameterized SQL scripts,
  ensuring consistent daily dataset refreshes and minimizing manual operational oversight.

- Conducted architectural reviews within Git repositories, mentoring engineers on modeling standards,
  code maintainability, and version control practices.

Environment: AWS, Amazon S3, AWS EMR, Amazon Redshift, AWS IAM, AWS Lambda, Amazon CloudWatch,
AWS CodePipeline, Palantir Foundry, Pipeline Builder, Foundry Workshop, PySpark, Python, pandas,
SQL, Git, REST APIs, Meta Ads API, ERP Systems, CRM Systems.


CIGNA HEALTH                                                                 Bloomfield, CT
Sr. Data Engineer                                                  August 2020 - May 2023

- Led analytics modernization on Microsoft Azure, converting healthcare claims and provider requirements
  into structured ETL pipelines within Palantir Foundry, implementing governance controls aligned with
  HIPAA controls to ensure secure patient data processing and regulatory compliance.

- Designed medallion architecture on Azure Data Lake Storage using PySpark, organizing claims,
  eligibility, and billing datasets into curated layers that supported actuarial modeling and downstream
  financial reporting.

- Integrated EHR Systems and CRM platforms through Azure Data Factory, orchestrating batch and
  incremental ingestion workflows with schema validation and data quality enforcement.

- Developed transformation logic using Python and pandas, enriching encounter records with derived cost
  and utilization metrics supporting operational monitoring and healthcare trend analysis.

- Built governed analytics applications in Foundry Workshop, enabling operations leaders to review
  denial patterns and service utilization through structured, self-service data interfaces.

- Implemented optimized data models in Azure Synapse Analytics, applying advanced SQL techniques to
  manage large member datasets and deliver timely executive-level reporting outputs.

- Configured role-based access by aligning Azure Active Directory roles with Palantir Foundry
  permissions, maintaining compliant and controlled access for analysts and clinical teams.

- Established CI/CD practices using Azure DevOps and Git, standardizing version control and deployment
  processes for ETL pipelines across collaborative engineering teams.

- Optimized cluster performance in Azure Databricks, tuning Spark configurations to balance compute
  efficiency with processing demands of high-volume claims datasets.

- Integrated marketing attribution datasets using Google Ads API, aligning campaign metrics with
  healthcare operational KPIs for unified analytics reporting.

Environment: Microsoft Azure, Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse Analytics,
Azure Databricks, Azure Active Directory, Azure DevOps, Azure Monitor, Palantir Foundry, Pipeline
Builder, Foundry Workshop, PySpark, Python, pandas, SQL, Git, Google Ads API, EHR Systems,
HIPAA Controls, REST APIs.


WORKDAY                                                                      Pleasanton, CA
Data Engineer / Data Analyst                                     October 2017 - July 2020

- Built foundational ETL pipelines on Google Cloud Platform, ingesting HR and finance datasets into
  Google Cloud Storage and transforming them through Palantir Foundry to support structured workforce
  analytics and standardized reporting processes.

- Designed medallion models using PySpark on Google Dataproc, organizing payroll, recruitment, and
  expense data into layered datasets that improved consistency and usability for HR and finance reporting.

- Developed transformation workflows in BigQuery, applying partitioning and clustering techniques with
  advanced SQL to improve performance for executive compensation and budgeting analysis.

- Integrated ERP Systems and HRIS Systems using secure REST APIs, ensuring reliable ingestion,
  consistent schema mapping, and controlled data exchange across cloud storage environments.

- Created validation routines with Python and pandas, reconciling payroll transactions and identifying
  discrepancies before financial reporting cycles.

- Implemented role-based access controls by aligning Palantir Foundry permissions with Google Cloud
  IAM, protecting confidential employee and compensation information.

- Automated recurring finance extracts through scheduled SQL workflows in BigQuery, reducing manual
  spreadsheet consolidation and improving reporting consistency.

- Built governed analytics applications in Foundry Workshop, enabling HR leaders to analyze hiring
  trends, attrition metrics, and workforce budgeting data interactively.

- Optimized distributed processing workloads in Google Dataproc by tuning PySpark memory settings and
  refining transformation logic for stable execution.

Environment: Google Cloud Platform, Google Cloud Storage, Google Dataproc, BigQuery, Google Cloud
IAM, Google Cloud Monitoring, Palantir Foundry, Pipeline Builder, Foundry Workshop, PySpark, Python,
pandas, SQL, Git, REST APIs, ERP Systems, HRIS Systems, Medallion Architecture.


PROGRESSIVE                                                             Mayfield Village, OH
Data Engineer / Migration Specialist                             December 2015 - September 2017

- Designed and implemented a robust Insurance Data Integration platform using AWS, improving data
  integration efficiency by 40% and ensuring seamless data flow across insurance systems.

- Developed and orchestrated data ingestion pipelines using AWS Glue and Amazon EMR, resulting in a
  30% increase in data ingestion efficiency from various insurance sources.

- Led data migration projects to transfer policyholder records from legacy systems to modern policy
  management platforms, ensuring data accuracy and continuity of coverage.

- Designed and implemented complex workflows using Apache Airflow, orchestrating ETL processes across
  distributed data systems for processing insurance analytics.

- Implemented Amazon RDS for structured data storage, optimizing SQL query performance and reducing
  response times by 25% for policy lookups and claims processing.

- Set up Amazon S3 for scalable storage of semi-structured and unstructured insurance data, enhancing
  data management capabilities for diverse policy documents and claim evidence.

- Developed and trained predictive models using Amazon SageMaker, increasing accuracy of risk
  assessment and fraud detection by 15% through advanced machine learning techniques.

- Designed and implemented interactive Tableau dashboards to visualize insurance metrics, improving
  decision-making processes and operational efficiency by 30%.

- Optimized query performance in Amazon Redshift by tuning SQL queries and implementing distribution
  keys and sort keys, resulting in a 25% improvement in query response times for insurance analytics.

Environment: Azure Cloud, Talend ETL, Tableau, Azure Data Factory, Azure Blob Storage, Azure Data
Lake Storage, Azure SQL Database, Azure Key Vault, Azure Active Directory, Azure Synapse Analytics,
Azure Databricks, Apache Kafka, Airflow, Snowflake, PySpark.


MASTEK                                                                        Mumbai, India
ETL Developer                                                       May 2014 - October 2015

- Designed and implemented a scalable ETL pipeline using AWS services, Informatica, and Talend to
  integrate data from diverse client systems, improving data availability by 40%.

- Configured AWS S3 for secure and efficient storage of raw, staging, and transformed data, enabling
  seamless data ingestion and transformation workflows for client projects.

- Utilized Informatica PowerCenter to extract data from various client databases, ensuring accurate and

- Developed Talend jobs to process and extract data from diverse file formats, achieving a 30%
  reduction in data processing time for client deliverables.

- Implemented complex data transformations using Informatica for data cleansing, enrichment, and

- Designed AWS Glue jobs to catalog and load transformed data into Amazon Redshift, optimizing data

- Automated ETL job scheduling and execution using AWS Lambda and Amazon CloudWatch Events, improving

- Ensured data security and compliance by implementing AWS IAM roles and policies, protecting sensitive

NITISH RAO B
SR. BIG DATA ENGINEER / AI-ML ENGINEER

LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/nitishraob  |  Phone: 4692696680  |  Email: nitishb2317@gmail.com


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

- Architected production machine learning pipelines integrating GPT-4, BERT, and LLaMA with spaCy
  and Hugging Face Transformers, orchestrating workflows through Apache Airflow and Kafka while
  deploying models across AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, and Google Cloud AI Platform with MLflow
  versioning.

- Developed end-to-end deep learning systems for LLM fine-tuning using PyTorch, TensorFlow, and ONNX
  Runtime, establishing data pipelines with Apache Spark, Databricks, and Delta Lake to process
  petabyte-scale datasets, implementing CI/CD through Jenkins, GitLab CI, and Kubernetes.

- Built NLP solutions leveraging GPT-4, T5, and BERT for text generation and sentiment analysis,
  designing data infrastructure with Python, Scala, and SQL across Snowflake, Redshift, and BigQuery,
  integrating vector databases including Pinecone, Weaviate, and Milvus for semantic search.

- Engineered ML pipeline architectures deploying LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Haystack frameworks,
  implementing data transformation workflows using Apache Flink, Presto, and dbt, establishing feature
  stores through Feast and Tecton with monitoring via Prometheus, Grafana, and Weights & Biases.

- Implemented RAG architectures combining LLMs with Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, and Redis, developing
  ETL processes through AWS Glue, Azure Data Factory, and Google Dataflow, establishing streaming
  pipelines using Kafka Streams and Apache Pulsar with governance frameworks via Apache Atlas.

- Executed domain-specific LLM fine-tuning using LoRA, QLoRA, and parameter-efficient methods,
  building lakehouse architectures with Trino, Apache Hudi, and Apache Iceberg, implementing
  distributed training through Horovod, DeepSpeed, and Ray with inference optimization using
  TensorRT and ONNX.

- Designed ML infrastructure deploying Docker, Kubernetes, and Helm for containerization, creating
  workflow management pipelines with Prefect, Dagster, and Luigi, implementing data lineage tracking
  through Marquez and DataHub while establishing A/B testing frameworks across multi-cloud hybrid
  infrastructure.

- Created conversational AI solutions using OpenAI API and Cohere, designing data integration systems
  with Apache NiFi, Talend, and Matillion, implementing real-time analytics through Apache Druid and
  ClickHouse with custom tokenizers for domain-specific vocabulary optimization.

- Built NLP pipelines incorporating prompt engineering and few-shot learning techniques, establishing
  polyglot persistence using PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Cassandra, implementing batch processing with
  Apache Samza and AWS Kinesis.

- Deployed semantic search and document understanding systems using Spacy, AllenNLP, and Stanford
  CoreNLP, orchestrating data ingestion workflows with Informatica, Apache Sqoop, and Fivetran,
  implementing data mesh architectures with model governance frameworks including bias detection and
  fairness metrics.

- Configured ML operations frameworks managing LLM lifecycle through Kubeflow, MLflow, and Amazon
  SageMaker Pipelines, establishing distributed caching using Memcached and Hazelcast with
  hyperparameter tuning via Optuna and Ray Tune.

- Maintained real-time data processing infrastructure using Apache Kafka, Apache Beam, and Apache
  Flink, deploying scalable cloud services across AWS SageMaker, Google Cloud AI Platform, and Azure
  ML, implementing feature engineering frameworks with Great Expectations and Pandera.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Databases & Tools        : MySQL, Teradata 15/14, Oracle 11g/10g, MS SQL Server 2014/2012, Erwin 8/9
Cloud Environment        : GCP (Cloud Storage, BigQuery, DataProc, Functions, GKE, Spanner)
                           AWS (Redshift, EMR, Glue, S3, Lambda, Athena, Snowflake, EC2, RDS,
                           CloudWatch, VPC, SQS, Jenkins)
                           Azure (Databricks, Data Lake, Data Factory, Blob Storage, SQL,
                           HDInsight, Fabric, CosmosDB)
Big Data Ecosystem       : Spark, Hive, Kafka, HDFS, Airflow, NiFi, Spark Streaming, Sqoop,
                           Flume, MapReduce, Yarn, Zookeeper
Programming Languages    : Python, Java, Scala, PySpark, Spring Boot, SQL, T-SQL, Unix
Reporting Tools          : Power BI, Tableau, EHR, Semarchy
ETL                      : Talend 5.6, AWS Glue, Informatica Power Center 10.x/9.6, SSIS,
                           Semarchy xDM
Python Libraries / ML    : Scikit-Learn, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Logistic Regression,
                           Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Decision Tree
Containerization         : Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, Docker Hub
Data Warehousing & BI    : Cloudera, Star and Snowflake Schema, SSIS, Facts and Dimensions, Splunk
Methodologies            : Agile, SDLC, Waterfall


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

UNITEDHEALTH GROUP                                                           Minnetonka, MN
Sr. Big Data Engineer                                             November 2022 - Current

- Architected production ML pipelines using Azure Machine Learning, Azure Databricks, and MLflow to
  support healthcare initiatives, integrating GPT-4 via Azure OpenAI and BERT, T5, LLaMA fine-tuned
  with LoRA and PEFT, extracting clinical insights from EHR data with HIPAA compliance.

- Built end-to-end NLP pipelines using Hugging Face Transformers, SpaCy, Sentence Transformers, and
  LangChain to process medical notes and radiology reports, implementing RAG architecture with Azure
  Cosmos DB Vector Search and Azure AI Search for contextually relevant patient histories.

- Developed scalable data engineering solutions using PySpark, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure Data
  Factory to ingest and transform multi-petabyte healthcare datasets, implementing Delta Lake for ACID
  transactions and using dbt for SQL-based transformations.

- Implemented LLM fine-tuning workflows using PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Azure AI Studio, applying PEFT
  and LoRA techniques to adapt BERT, GPT-3.5, and T5 for medical entity recognition and ICD-10 coding,
  deploying via Azure Kubernetes Service with Docker containers.

- Orchestrated real-time ML monitoring using Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Prometheus, and
  Grafana, tracking model drift and data quality anomalies, implementing SHAP and LIME for
  explainability to satisfy clinical validation requirements under HIPAA and HITECH frameworks.

- Engineered feature stores using Feast on Azure Databricks to centralize reusable features for ML
  pipelines, integrating Pandas, NumPy, and PySpark for batch and streaming computation, enforcing
  HIPAA-compliant access policies through Azure Active Directory.

- Built adverse event detection systems using Azure Cognitive Services, SpaCy, and Sentence
  Transformers to analyze patient safety reports, implementing LLMs fine-tuned with Hugging Face
  Transformers and LoRA to classify safety signals.

- Deployed production ML pipelines using Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions to automate model training and
  deployment workflows, integrating MLflow for experiment tracking and DVC for dataset versioning, using
  Terraform for infrastructure-as-code.

- Developed clinical prediction models using Python, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, and LightGBM for patient
  readmission risk and sepsis early warning, training models on Azure Machine Learning with PySpark for
  distributed processing.

- Integrated RAG frameworks using LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Azure Cosmos DB Vector Search to support
  clinical question-answering systems, enabling physicians to query evidence-based recommendations while
  adhering to HIPAA and HL7 FHIR APIs.

- Fine-tuned LLMs including GPT-4, BERT, and T5 using PyTorch, TensorFlow, and PEFT techniques for
  clinical summarization and medical coding automation, validating performance using SHAP and LIME.

- Deployed containerized NLP services using Docker, Azure Kubernetes Service, and Azure Service Bus
  for asynchronous processing of clinical text data, implementing autoscaling policies based on
  Prometheus and Grafana monitoring.

Environment: Azure Machine Learning, Azure Databricks, MLflow, GPT-4, Azure OpenAI, BERT, T5, LLaMA,
LoRA, PEFT, Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2, Delta Lake, Hugging Face Transformers, SpaCy, LangChain,
PySpark, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Azure Kubernetes Service,
Docker, Prometheus, Grafana, SHAP, LIME, Feast, Pandas, NumPy, Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions,
Terraform, DVC, Python, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, LlamaIndex.


WELLS FARGO                                                              San Francisco, CA
Sr. Data Engineer                                               January 2020 - October 2022

- Designed production ML pipelines for credit risk modeling and fraud detection using Amazon SageMaker
  Pipelines integrated with AWS Step Functions, orchestrating workflows from Amazon Kinesis Data
  Streams to deployment with SageMaker Model Monitor, maintaining PCI-DSS compliance via AWS IAM.

- Built Large Language Models for financial document analysis using Amazon SageMaker Studio and
  SageMaker Autopilot, processing unstructured text from Amazon S3, integrating Amazon Comprehend for
  entity recognition and Amazon Textract for document extraction, ensuring SOX audit trails and GLBA
  data privacy requirements.

- Engineered scalable data infrastructure by designing AWS Glue ETL workflows extracting financial
  transaction data from Amazon RDS and Amazon Aurora into Amazon Redshift, implementing AWS Glue Data
  Catalog and AWS Lake Formation for metadata management with PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance.

- Developed real-time fraud detection systems using LLMs and NLP within Amazon SageMaker, processing
  streaming data through Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose and Amazon MSK, creating pipelines incorporating
  SageMaker Feature Store for feature engineering.

- Implemented customer behavior prediction solutions using Amazon Personalize and Amazon Forecast,
  trained on historical data processed through Apache Spark on EMR clusters, managing versioning in
  SageMaker Model Monitor while ensuring GLBA privacy standards.

- Deployed anti-money laundering detection infrastructure using Amazon SageMaker ensemble models
  integrated with Amazon OpenSearch Service for real-time alerting, configuring SageMaker Autopilot to
  explore model architectures, utilizing SageMaker Clarify for explainability.

- Engineered feature workflows for LLMs and ML pipelines using SageMaker Feature Store, implementing
  data transformations using AWS Glue PySpark jobs processing structured data from Amazon Redshift and
  unstructured text from Amazon S3, maintaining GLBA compliance through data masking and encryption.

- Established CI/CD pipelines for model deployment using AWS CodePipeline integrated with AWS
  CodeBuild for automated testing, implementing automated approval gates requiring SageMaker Clarify
  bias reports before production deployment.

- Built scalable infrastructure for training pipelines by configuring Amazon EMR clusters with Apache
  Spark for distributed feature engineering, processing petabyte-scale financial datasets.

Environment: Amazon SageMaker Pipelines, AWS Step Functions, Amazon Kinesis, SageMaker Model Monitor,
AWS CodePipeline, AWS CodeBuild, AWS IAM, Amazon SageMaker Studio, SageMaker Autopilot, Amazon S3,
Amazon Comprehend, Amazon Textract, AWS Glue, Amazon RDS, Amazon Aurora, Amazon Redshift, AWS Lake
Formation, Amazon CloudWatch, SageMaker Feature Store, SageMaker Clarify, AWS Lambda, Amazon ECS,
Amazon Personalize, Amazon Forecast, Apache Spark, EMR, Amazon Lex, Amazon Kendra, AWS CloudFormation.


HONEYWELL                                                                    Charlotte, NC
Data Engineer / Data Analyst                                     March 2017 - December 2019

- Designed production ML pipelines on Google Cloud Platform for predictive maintenance using
  TensorFlow and Scikit-learn to build anomaly detection models analyzing sensor data, orchestrating
  workflows with Cloud Composer for feature extraction and model retraining.

- Built Large Language Models inference pipelines using Python and TensorFlow to process unstructured
  maintenance logs and equipment manuals, applying NLP techniques including tokenization and entity
  recognition, integrating Cloud Pub/Sub for real-time ingestion.

- Implemented data infrastructure on GCP consolidating IoT sensor telemetry and SCADA systems into
  BigQuery as central data warehouse, developing Python-based ETL pipelines leveraging Pandas and
  NumPy for transformation and validation.

- Developed time-series forecasting solutions for equipment failures using XGBoost and TensorFlow,
  performing feature engineering with Pandas and NumPy, establishing production pipelines with
  automated hyperparameter tuning orchestrated through Cloud Composer.

- Constructed NLP models using TensorFlow and Scikit-learn to classify maintenance work orders from
  free-text descriptions, applying LLM fine-tuning techniques to domain-specific vocabulary.

- Managed real-time equipment monitoring systems by designing streaming architectures using Cloud
  Pub/Sub and Cloud Dataflow with Apache Beam for windowed aggregations, storing time-series data in
  Cloud Bigtable and Cloud SQL.

- Engineered production pipelines for quality control image classification using TensorFlow
  convolutional neural networks with transfer learning, automating training workflows using Kubeflow
  Pipelines on GKE.

- Deployed XGBoost gradient boosting models for demand forecasting and inventory optimization,
  integrating Pandas for preprocessing, operationalizing models through scheduled Cloud Composer
  workflows.

Environment: Google Cloud Platform, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, Cloud Composer, Airflow, Kubeflow,
Google Kubernetes Engine, Python, NLP, Cloud Pub/Sub, Cloud Dataflow, Apache Beam, BigQuery, Pandas,
NumPy, Cloud Dataproc, Spark, PySpark, XGBoost, TensorBoard, Stackdriver, Cloud IAM, Cloud Storage,
Cloud Bigtable, Cloud SQL, Cloud Spanner, SQL.


EQUIFAX                                                                        Atlanta, GA
Data Engineer / Migration Specialist                              February 2016 - February 2017

- Built robust Clinical Data Integration platform using Azure Cloud, improving data integration
  efficiency. Reduced integration time for financial data sources by 65%.

- Developed and managed data ingestion pipelines using Talend ETL and Azure Data Factory from clinical
  sources. Processed over 2TB of consumer credit data daily with 99.9% reliability.

- Executed data migration projects transferring patient records from legacy systems to new EHR
  platforms, successfully migrating 50 million consumer records with zero data loss.

- Created and built complex workflows using Apache Airflow orchestrating ETL processes across
  distributed data systems. Implemented automated error handling reducing manual intervention by 85%.

- Applied Azure SQL Database for structured data storage, improving query performance by 70% through
  advanced indexing strategies.

- Established Azure Data Lake Storage for scalable storage of semi-structured and unstructured clinical
  data, enabling analysts to access credit information 3x faster.

- Applied data security and privacy measures complying with HIPAA regulations while working with FHIR
  and OMOP data models.

- Built and refined predictive models using Azure Databricks, achieving 87% accuracy in credit risk
  scoring models.

- Implemented real-time data validation frameworks ensuring high data quality across all ingestion
  pipelines. Reduced data quality incidents by 78% through automated validation checks.

Environment: Azure Cloud, Talend ETL, Azure Data Factory, Apache Airflow, Azure SQL Database, Azure
Data Lake Storage, HIPAA, FHIR, OMOP, HL7, Azure Databricks, Machine Learning, Data Validation,
Data Migration, Data Security, Encryption.


INNO MINDS                                                                     Pune, India
ETL Developer                                                       May 2014 - December 2015

- Created scalable ETL pipeline using AWS services, Informatica, and Talend combining data from
  relational, NoSQL databases, and flat files. Processed over 500GB of daily manufacturing data from
  12 different source systems with 99.8% accuracy.

- Set up AWS S3 storing raw, staging, and transformed data securely, implementing bucket policies and
  encryption ensuring regulatory compliance.

- Used Informatica PowerCenter extracting data from MySQL, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB databases,
  developing custom connectors for legacy manufacturing systems reducing integration complexity by 40%.

- Created Talend jobs processing and extracting data from CSV and JSON files, decreasing batch window
  from 6 hours to under 2 hours.

- Built AWS Glue jobs cataloging and loading transformed data into Redshift, implementing partitioning
  strategies improving query performance by 300% for manufacturing analytics.

- Set up automated ETL job scheduling using AWS Lambda and CloudWatch Events, creating self-healing
  pipelines automatically retrying failed jobs reducing manual interventions by 90%.

- Connected Tableau to AWS Redshift creating interactive dashboards showing manufacturing KPIs enabling
  data-driven decisions that improved production efficiency by 22%.

Environment: AWS S3, AWS Glue, AWS Lambda, AWS CloudWatch, AWS Redshift, Informatica PowerCenter,
Talend Open Studio, Tableau, MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, ETL Pipelines, Data Transformation.


EDUCATION

Sreenidhi Institute of Science and Technology
Bachelor's in Computer and Information Science                       August 2010 - May 2014

text:
SOHITH YALAMATI
SR. JAVA FULL STACK DEVELOPER


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

- Developed and maintained full-stack web applications with over 6 years of experience, demonstrating
  expertise in both front-end and back-end technologies. Implemented complex full-stack solutions,
  ensuring seamless integration between UI components and server-side logic while adhering to best
  practices in software architecture.

- Designed and built responsive and high-performing UIs using React and Angular (v13+), creating
  intuitive user experiences across multiple platforms and devices.

- Engineered secure and scalable back-end services using Java and Spring Boot, developing robust
  RESTful APIs to support complex business logic and data processing.

- Demonstrated proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript, utilizing these languages to create dynamic
  and interactive front-end components.

- Implemented microservices architecture to develop modular and scalable applications, ensuring high
  availability and fault tolerance.

- Utilized SQL Server, DB2, and NoSQL databases including MongoDB to design and implement efficient
  data storage solutions.

- Gained extensive experience with AWS cloud services, implementing scalable and cost-effective
  solutions for web applications.

- Collaborated with DevOps teams to implement CI/CD processes using tools such as Jenkins, GitHub
  Actions, and GitLab CI, streamlining deployment workflows and automating testing procedures.

- Demonstrated expertise in Docker and Kubernetes, containerizing applications and orchestrating
  deployments across multiple environments.

- Applied Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) methodologies to ensure
  high-quality code and reduce defects.

- Participated actively in Agile/Scrum ceremonies, contributing to sprint planning, daily stand-ups,
  and retrospectives.

- Conducted thorough code reviews to maintain code quality, identify potential issues, and share
  knowledge within the development team.

- Gained valuable experience in the healthcare and insurance industries, applying domain knowledge to
  develop tailored software solutions.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Programming Languages    : Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, SQL
Frontend Frameworks      : React, Angular
Backend Frameworks       : Spring Boot
Databases                : SQL Server, DB2, MongoDB, NoSQL
Cloud & DevOps           : AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI
Code Quality             : SonarQube
Version Control          : GitHub, GitLab
API Architecture         : REST APIs, Microservices
Development Practices    : CI/CD, TDD, BDD
Methodologies            : Agile, Scrum


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

FANNIE MAE
Sr. Java Full Stack Developer

- Designed and implemented a high-performance Java microservices architecture for a financial trading
  platform, utilizing Spring Boot 3.2 for rapid development and scalability. Integrated Java 17 features
  to enhance code efficiency and readability.

- Developed responsive front-end interfaces using React 18 and TypeScript 5.0, implementing real-time
  data visualization for financial analytics dashboards.

- Engineered a robust SQL Server 2022 database schema to support complex financial transactions and
  reporting requirements. Optimized SQL queries and implemented indexing strategies, reducing average
  response times significantly.

- Implemented containerization using Docker and orchestrated deployments with Kubernetes, ensuring
  consistent environments across development, testing, and production.

- Developed and maintained CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitHub Actions, automating build, test,
  and deployment processes. Integrated SonarQube for continuous code quality analysis.

- Created a real-time transaction monitoring system using Java streams and MongoDB change streams,
  enabling instant detection of suspicious financial activities.

- Designed and implemented a secure OAuth 2.0 authentication system using Spring Security, ensuring
  robust access control for sensitive financial data. Integrated JWT tokens for stateless authentication.

- Developed a high-performance Angular 15 single-page application for portfolio management, utilizing
  RxJS for reactive programming and NgRx for state management.

- Migrated legacy financial applications from monolithic architecture to microservices using Spring Boot
  and AWS services. Utilized AWS Lambda for serverless computing and AWS S3 for secure document storage.

- Implemented a NoSQL data storage solution using MongoDB Atlas for handling unstructured financial data,
  enabling flexible schema design and horizontal scaling.

- Developed a real-time market data processing system using Java streams and Kafka, enabling low-latency
  data ingestion and analysis.

- Created a comprehensive test suite using JUnit 5 and Mockito for unit and integration testing of Java
  services. Implemented Selenium WebDriver for automated UI testing of React and Angular components.

- Implemented a GitLab CI pipeline for continuous integration and delivery, automating build, test, and
  deployment processes for microservices.

- Developed a custom Spring Boot actuator for enhanced monitoring and health checks of microservices.
  Integrated with Prometheus and Grafana for real-time performance monitoring and alerting.

- Implemented a data migration strategy from legacy DB2 systems to SQL Server 2022, ensuring data
  integrity and minimal downtime. Developed custom ETL processes using Java and Spring Batch.

- Engineered a fraud detection system utilizing machine learning algorithms and DB2 for data storage.
  Implemented real-time transaction monitoring and anomaly detection, reducing fraudulent activities
  by 40%.

Environment: Java, Spring Boot, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Angular, SQL Server, DB2, NoSQL,
MongoDB, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, SonarQube, REST APIs,
Microservices, CI/CD, TDD, BDD, Agile, Scrum.


FIFTH-THIRD BANK
Sr. Java Full Stack Developer

- Designed and implemented a robust banking transaction system using Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.0,
  incorporating real-time fraud detection algorithms and integrating with SQL Server for secure data
  storage.

- Developed a high-performance frontend for online banking services using React 18 and TypeScript 4.9,
  implementing responsive design principles and accessibility features.

- Architected and deployed a microservices-based core banking system on AWS using Docker and Kubernetes,
  ensuring scalability and fault tolerance. Implemented CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins and GitHub Actions.

- Engineered a real-time payment processing system using Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.0, integrating with
  MongoDB for high-speed transaction logging. Implemented event-driven architecture using Apache Kafka
  for asynchronous processing.

- Developed a secure authentication and authorization system for banking applications using Spring
  Security and OAuth 2.0, integrating with Active Directory for employee access management.

- Created a dynamic reporting dashboard using Angular 15 and TypeScript 4.9, visualizing complex
  financial data with D3.js.

- Optimized database performance by fine-tuning SQL queries and implementing indexing strategies in SQL
  Server and DB2. Developed stored procedures and triggers to ensure data integrity.

- Implemented a distributed caching solution using Redis and Spring Cache, significantly reducing
  database load and improving response times for frequently accessed banking data.

- Developed and maintained GitLab CI pipelines for continuous integration and deployment, incorporating
  SonarQube for code quality analysis.

- Designed and implemented a fault-tolerant, high-availability architecture for critical banking
  services using AWS services such as EC2, RDS, and Lambda.

- Implemented real-time data synchronization between on-premises systems and cloud-based services
  using Apache Kafka and AWS Kinesis, ensuring data consistency across distributed banking applications.

- Developed a machine learning-based fraud detection system using Python and TensorFlow, integrating
  it with Java-based banking applications through RESTful APIs.

- Implemented a comprehensive logging and monitoring solution using the ELK stack for banking
  applications, enabling real-time insights and proactive issue resolution.

- Developed a machine learning model for predicting credit risk using historical data stored in DB2 and
  NoSQL databases. Improved accuracy of credit assessments by 15%.

- Designed and developed a dispute resolution system using React and Node.js to streamline the process
  of handling consumer credit report disputes.

Environment: Java, Spring Boot, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Angular, SQL Server, DB2, NoSQL,
MongoDB, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, SonarQube, REST APIs,
Microservices, CI/CD, TDD, BDD, Agile, Scrum.


EQUIFAX
Sr. Java Full Stack Developer

- Designed and implemented a robust credit scoring system using Java 11 and Spring Boot 2.5, integrating
  machine learning algorithms to analyze vast datasets from multiple sources, improving accuracy of
  credit risk assessments.

- Developed a real-time credit monitoring dashboard using React 17 and TypeScript 4, incorporating
  responsive design principles and integrating with RESTful APIs built on Spring Boot.

- Engineered a high-performance data pipeline using AWS Lambda and S3, processing terabytes of
  financial data daily, while ensuring data integrity and compliance with FCRA regulations.

- Implemented a microservices architecture using Docker and Kubernetes, facilitating seamless scaling
  and deployment of credit reporting services, while leveraging Jenkins for continuous integration.

- Developed a secure authentication system using OAuth 2.0 and JWT tokens, integrating with Active
  Directory for user management.

- Optimized complex SQL queries in SQL Server and DB2, improving query performance through restructuring
  indexing strategies and implementing database partitioning.

- Created a dispute resolution module using Angular 12 and TypeScript, integrating with backend Java
  services to streamline the process of handling consumer disputes.

- Designed and implemented a NoSQL data store using MongoDB for handling unstructured data in credit
  reports, enabling faster retrieval of varied data types.

- Utilized AWS CloudFormation to automate infrastructure provisioning, ensuring consistent and
  repeatable deployments across multiple environments, while implementing AWS CloudWatch for monitoring.

- Implemented code quality checks using SonarQube and integrated it with GitHub Actions.

- Developed a batch processing system using Spring Batch, efficiently handling large volumes of credit
  data updates, while implementing retry mechanisms and error handling.

- Designed and implemented a caching layer using Redis, significantly reducing database load and
  improving response times for frequently accessed credit information.

- Implemented GitLab CI pipelines for automated testing and deployment, incorporating security scans
  and performance tests.

- Developed a data masking solution using Java and Spring Boot, ensuring sensitive information is
  properly anonymized in non-production environments.

- Optimized database performance and query execution times for large-scale credit history searches,
  improving response times for credit inquiries by 40%.

- Engineered a fraud detection system utilizing NoSQL databases and MongoDB to store and analyze large
  volumes of transaction data. Implemented machine learning algorithms to identify suspicious patterns
  in real-time.

- Created a RESTful API using Node.js and Express.js to facilitate secure data exchange between credit
  bureau systems and third-party financial institutions. Implemented OAuth 2.0 authentication and rate
  limiting.

Environment: Java, Spring Boot, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Angular, SQL Server, DB2, NoSQL,
MongoDB, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, SonarQube, REST APIs,
Microservices, CI/CD, TDD, BDD, Agile, Scrum.


PROJECT SUMMARY

Client 1: Fannie Mae
  Frontend   : React 18
  Backend    : Java 17, Spring Boot 3.2
  Cloud      : AWS, AWS Lambda, S3
  Database   : SQL Databases
  Testing    : JUnit 5 and Mockito
  Deployment : Docker and Kubernetes
  Monitoring : Prometheus and Grafana

Client 2: Fifth-Third Bank
  Frontend   : React 18, TypeScript 4.9, React Hooks, Context API
  Backend    : Java
  Cloud      : AWS
  Database   : SQL Server, DB2, MongoDB
  Testing    : JUnit 5 and Mockito
  Deployment : Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, SonarQube

Client 3: Equifax
  Frontend   : Angular
  Backend    : Java 11, Spring Boot 2.5
  Cloud      : AWS
  Database   : SQL
  API        : Node.js/Express.js API, RESTful APIs
  Deployment : GitLab CI, Jenkins, GitHub Actions
