text:
RAGHAVENDRA PRASAD VEMURI
SENIOR CLOUD ARCHITECT | ENTERPRISE CLOUD TRANSFORMATION LEADER | DEVOPS & INFRASTRUCTURE STRATEGIST

Location: Dallas, Texas
Phone: +1 (972) 555-8432
Email: raghav.vemuri.cloud@gmail.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/raghavendra-cloud
GitHub: github.com/raghav-cloud


EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

- 13+ years of IT experience with 8+ years specializing in Cloud Architecture, Infrastructure
  Modernization, and Enterprise DevOps Transformation across Banking and FinTech domains.

- Proven expertise in architecting secure, scalable, and high-availability cloud-native systems on AWS.

- Extensive experience designing enterprise cloud migration strategies including re-platforming,
  containerization, and microservices modernization.

- Architected distributed systems supporting millions of financial transactions daily.

- Strong background in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation), CI/CD pipelines, and
  Kubernetes-based orchestration.

- Designed multi-account AWS environments with centralized security governance and compliance controls.

- Led FinOps initiatives reducing cloud spend by over 28%.

- Deep expertise in VPC architecture, IAM governance, disaster recovery planning, and zero-downtime
  deployments.

- Experienced in mentoring cross-functional engineering teams and establishing cloud standards across
  enterprise platforms.


CORE COMPETENCIES

Enterprise Cloud Architecture, AWS Infrastructure Strategy, Microservices & Containerization,
DevOps & CI/CD Architecture, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), High Availability & Disaster Recovery,
Cloud Security & Compliance, Cost Optimization (FinOps), API & Event-Driven Architecture,
Observability & Monitoring Strategy.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Cloud Platforms          : AWS (EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, DynamoDB, ECS, EKS, IAM, VPC,
                           CloudFront, Route53, CloudWatch)
Infrastructure as Code   : Terraform, AWS CloudFormation
Containers & Orchestration : Docker, Kubernetes (EKS)
CI/CD Tools              : Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI
Programming & Scripting  : Python, Java, Bash, SQL
Databases                : PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle, DynamoDB, MongoDB
Monitoring & Logging     : Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, CloudWatch
Security & Compliance    : IAM Policies, SCP, SSL/TLS, Encryption at Rest & Transit, OAuth2
Networking               : VPC Peering, Transit Gateway, Direct Connect, VPN, NACLs, Security
                           Groups, PrivateLink
Serverless & Event-Driven: API Gateway, SNS, SQS, EventBridge, Step Functions
DevSecOps & Compliance   : AWS Config, GuardDuty, Security Hub, SAST/DAST Integration,
                           Secrets Manager
High Availability & DR   : Multi-AZ Deployments, Cross-Region Replication, Backup & Restore,
                           RTO/RPO Planning
FinOps & Cost Optimization: AWS Cost Explorer, Reserved Instances, Spot Instances, Rightsizing,
                           Intelligent Tiering
Microservices & API      : RESTful APIs, API Gateway Design, Service Mesh (Istio), Contract-First
                           API Design
Observability & SRE      : Distributed Tracing, Log Aggregation, Alerting Strategy, SLA/SLO
                           Definition, Chaos Testing


CLOUD ARCHITECTURE EXPERTISE

AWS Enterprise Architecture Design

- Designed multi-tier VPC architecture with secure subnet segmentation.
- Implemented cross-account access controls and centralized IAM governance.
- Designed HA systems across multiple availability zones.
- Architected serverless solutions using Lambda & API Gateway.
- Designed centralized logging and observability strategy.
- Implemented AWS Control Tower for centralized account provisioning and compliance enforcement.
- Architected multi-region active-active and active-passive failover strategies.
- Designed network segmentation strategy using Transit Gateway and VPC peering.
- Implemented PrivateLink architecture for secure service-to-service communication.
- Designed scalable event-driven systems using SNS, SQS, and EventBridge.
- Established centralized identity federation using SSO and IAM role assumptions.
- Architected secure API architecture with WAF and Shield for DDoS protection.
- Designed zero-trust network model using strict security group and NACL controls.
- Implemented infrastructure resilience testing including chaos engineering simulations.

DevOps & Automation Strategy

- Automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform.
- Built fully automated CI/CD pipelines for containerized applications.
- Integrated security scanning and compliance validation in pipelines.
- Implemented blue-green and rolling deployment strategies.
- Designed GitOps-based deployment workflows using branch-based infrastructure control.
- Integrated automated container image scanning using Trivy and ECR scanning.
- Implemented Terraform modules for reusable infrastructure components.
- Automated multi-environment provisioning (DEV, QA, UAT, PROD) with configuration isolation.
- Implemented Canary deployments to reduce release risk.
- Designed CI/CD rollback mechanisms for zero-downtime recovery.
- Integrated SonarQube and SAST tools for code quality enforcement.
- Designed centralized secrets management using AWS Secrets Manager.
- Automated database schema migrations as part of deployment workflows.


ENTERPRISE CLOUD TRANSFORMATION

Cloud Migration Program

- Led migration of 35+ on-prem applications to AWS.
- Designed re-architecture for monolithic systems into microservices.
- Implemented RDS migration from legacy Oracle databases.
- Designed disaster recovery with cross-region replication.

FinOps & Cost Optimization

- Reduced EC2 cost through rightsizing initiatives.
- Implemented auto-scaling policies for dynamic workload handling.
- Designed reserved instance planning strategy.
- Reduced overall cloud spend by 28%.


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

GOLDMAN SACHS                                                                  Dallas, TX
Senior Cloud Architect                                                 April 2020 - Present

Project: Modernization of enterprise trading platform to cloud-native AWS infrastructure.

Cloud Architecture
- Designed multi-account AWS landing zone.
- Implemented secure VPC peering and network segmentation.
- Designed Kubernetes (EKS) clusters for container workloads.

Microservices & Containers
- Containerized 25+ services using Docker.
- Implemented auto-scaling and high availability policies.

Security & Compliance
- Designed IAM policies following least privilege principles.
- Implemented encryption using AWS KMS.

DevOps & CI/CD
- Integrated Terraform into automated deployment pipelines.
- Reduced deployment time by 45%.

Monitoring & Reliability
- Implemented centralized monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana.
- Achieved 99.99% uptime SLA.

Environment: AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Docker, PostgreSQL, Jenkins.


JPMORGAN CHASE                                                                 Chicago, IL
Cloud Solutions Engineer                                           January 2016 - March 2020

- Designed AWS infrastructure for customer-facing banking applications.
- Migrated legacy systems to containerized microservices.
- Implemented API Gateway-based secure integrations.
- Automated infrastructure provisioning using CloudFormation.
- Designed Redis caching architecture to improve performance by 35%.
- Designed multi-tier VPC architecture with public/private subnets and NAT gateways for secure
  internet access.
- Implemented auto-scaling groups and load balancers to handle peak banking transaction traffic.
- Configured AWS IAM roles and policies following least-privilege security principles.
- Implemented RDS multi-AZ deployments ensuring high availability and failover capability.
- Designed centralized logging solution using CloudWatch Logs and CloudTrail for compliance tracking.
- Implemented encryption for data at rest using AWS KMS and SSL/TLS for data in transit.
- Designed blue-green deployment strategy to minimize downtime during application releases.
- Optimized EC2 instance sizing and storage allocation reducing operational cost by 18%.
- Conducted disaster recovery testing and validated backup restoration processes across environments.

Environment: AWS, EC2, S3, RDS, Redis, IAM, ELB, Auto Scaling, CloudFormation, Jenkins.


EXPERIAN                                                                       Atlanta, GA
Cloud Infrastructure Engineer                                        June 2012 - December 2015

- Supported hybrid cloud integration strategy.
- Designed secure VPC architecture and security groups.
- Implemented S3 backup and disaster recovery solutions.
- Built CloudWatch dashboards for infrastructure monitoring.
- Implemented EC2 provisioning and AMI management for standardized infrastructure builds.
- Configured security groups and network ACLs to enforce layered security controls.
- Automated infrastructure setup using AWS CLI and scripting tools.
- Designed S3 lifecycle policies for archival and cost optimization.
- Implemented CloudWatch alarms and SNS notifications for proactive monitoring.
- Assisted in migration of on-prem SQL Server databases to AWS RDS.
- Configured Elastic Load Balancers for high availability of web applications.
- Implemented backup strategies using AWS snapshots and cross-region replication.
- Supported infrastructure patch management and OS hardening initiatives.
- Performed root cause analysis for production incidents and implemented preventive measures.

Environment: AWS EC2, S3, RDS, SQL Server, CloudWatch, IAM, Jenkins.


MAJOR CLOUD PROGRAMS

Enterprise AWS Modernization Initiative

- Migrated 35+ applications to AWS.
- Improved system scalability and resilience.
- Reduced infrastructure provisioning time by 50%.
- Conducted application dependency mapping and migration readiness assessments.
- Designed phased migration roadmap minimizing production disruption.
- Implemented database replication strategy using AWS DMS.
- Migrated legacy file storage systems to S3 with lifecycle management.
- Implemented containerization strategy to modernize legacy Java applications.
- Designed hybrid connectivity using Direct Connect and VPN tunnels.
- Established cloud governance model including tagging strategy and compliance standards.
- Designed API-first modernization strategy for legacy systems.

Kubernetes Containerization Program

- Containerized legacy services.
- Implemented auto-scaling policies.
- Reduced environment inconsistencies by 70%.
- Implemented AWS Cost Explorer dashboards for granular cost visibility.
- Automated shutdown of idle non-production environments.
- Implemented spot instance strategies for batch workloads.
- Optimized S3 storage classes using Intelligent Tiering policies.
- Optimized RDS instance sizing reducing database costs by 22%.
- Implemented predictive scaling strategies based on historical workload patterns.


MEASURABLE IMPACT

- Reduced cloud infrastructure cost by 28%
- Achieved 99.99% availability SLA
- Reduced deployment cycle time by 45%
- Migrated 35+ enterprise applications
- Improved DR recovery time by 40%


EDUCATION

University of Texas at Dallas
Master of Science in Cloud Computing                                          2015 - 2017

Osmania University
Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science                                   2008 - 2012


CERTIFICATIONS

AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Professional
AWS Certified DevOps Engineer
Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
HashiCorp Terraform Associate

FATIMA AL-RASHIDI
QA Lead | Cypress | TypeScript | Healthcare IT | FHIR | Compliance

fatima.alrashidi.qa@gmail.com | +1 (713) 882-4401 | Houston, TX
linkedin.com/in/fatimaalrashidi-qa | 10+ Years Experience

Ensuring clinical data integrity and regulatory compliance for healthcare platforms serving millions of patients


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Results-driven QA Lead with 10+ years of specialized expertise in healthcare IT quality assurance covering Electronic Health Records (EHR), clinical decision support, and patient engagement platforms at Cerner (Oracle Health), HCA Healthcare, and Deloitte. Expert in HL7 FHIR R4 API testing, HIPAA compliance automation, and clinical workflow validation using Cypress, Playwright, and REST Assured. Deep domain knowledge across Epic EHR, Cerner Millennium, and cloud-native digital health platforms. Built comprehensive automated HIPAA compliance test suites, PHI data masking validation frameworks, and Section 508 accessibility testing programs for government health agency clients. Proven expertise in CMS interoperability rule compliance (21st Century Cures Act), prior authorization API testing, and clinical quality measure (CQM) validation. HIPAA Security Officer certification and CPHIMS (Certified Professional in Health Informatics and Information Management) holder.


CORE COMPETENCIES

Test Automation: Cypress 13+, Playwright 1.40+, Selenium WebDriver, REST Assured, Karate DSL

Languages: TypeScript, Java, Python, SQL, HL7 FHIR Path, CQL (Clinical Quality Language)

Healthcare APIs: HL7 FHIR R4 (SMART on FHIR, CDS Hooks), HL7 v2 ADT/ORM/ORU, IHE Profiles (PIX/PDQ/XDS)

Compliance: HIPAA Privacy & Security, 21st Century Cures Act, CMS Interoperability Rule, ADA Section 508

Clinical Standards: ICD-10-CM/PCS, CPT-4, LOINC, SNOMED CT, RxNorm, NCQA HEDIS Measures

CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Azure Pipelines, Docker, SonarQube

Cloud: AWS (HealthLake, S3, Lambda, Comprehend Medical), Azure Health Data Services

Database: Oracle, SQL Server, MongoDB, FHIR Server (HAPI FHIR) Query Testing

Security: OWASP Healthcare, OAuth 2.0/SMART Auth, FHIR Bulk Data, Break-the-Glass Access Testing

Test Management: JIRA, TestRail, Azure DevOps, HP ALM, Confluence


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

CERNER CORPORATION (ORACLE HEALTH) — Kansas City, MO (Remote)
QA Automation Lead – FHIR Platform | Apr 2020–Present

QA Automation Lead for Cerner's HL7 FHIR R4 API platform and CernerWorks cloud EHR services supporting 27,000 facilities and 1 billion+ patient records globally.

- Architected HL7 FHIR R4 API automation framework using Karate DSL covering all 146 FHIR resource types including Patient, Observation, Condition, MedicationRequest, and DiagnosticReport.
- Designed SMART on FHIR authorization test suite validating standalone launch, EHR launch, and backend service flows across OAuth 2.0 and PKCE grant types.
- Built automated 21st Century Cures Act (USCDI) compliance validation ensuring Cerner's APIs met ONC certification requirements for data blocking prohibition.
- Implemented automated PHI data masking validation ensuring all test environments correctly de-identified patient data per HIPAA Safe Harbor method.
- Designed automated CDS Hooks (Clinical Decision Support) integration testing validating clinical decision alerts for drug-drug interactions, allergy warnings, and clinical guidelines.
- Built automated CQM (Clinical Quality Measure) calculation validation comparing Cerner's eCQM engine output to independently computed benchmark values.
- Implemented automated prior authorization API testing per CMS Interoperability Rule requirements validating PDex Plan Net and Da Vinci guides.
- Designed FHIR Bulk Data export testing validating Group-level and System-level exports for population health data access.
- Automated HL7 v2 ADT message processing testing validating patient registration, admit, transfer, and discharge event handling.
- Built automated NCQA HEDIS measure calculation validation for preventive care quality reporting.
- Implemented automated Break-the-Glass emergency access testing ensuring audit logging and supervisor notification workflows.
- Reduced regression cycle for FHIR API releases from 3 weeks to 4 days enabling monthly certified API releases.
- Led 6-member QA team including 2 FHIR API specialists and 4 automation engineers.
- Built automated IHE XDS (Cross-Enterprise Document Sharing) integration tests for Cerner's EHR document repository.
- Designed automated medication reconciliation workflow validation testing allergy checking, formulary adherence, and drug dosing alerts.

Environment: Karate DSL, Cypress, TypeScript, REST Assured, Java, HAPI FHIR Server, Oracle DB, GitHub Actions, Azure Health Data Services, Jenkins


HCA HEALTHCARE — Nashville, TN
Senior QA Automation Engineer | May 2016–Mar 2020

Senior automation engineer for HCA's patient portal, clinical documentation, and revenue cycle management platforms across 180+ hospitals.

- Built Selenium automation framework for MyHealthONE patient portal covering appointment scheduling, medical records access, bill pay, and care team messaging.
- Designed REST API automation for HCA's FHIR-enabled patient data access APIs using REST Assured.
- Implemented automated HIPAA access control testing validating role-based data visibility for clinicians, nurses, administrative staff, and patients.
- Conducted JMeter performance testing for HCA's patient portal simulating 500,000 concurrent patient logins during COVID-19 vaccine scheduling surge.
- Automated revenue cycle workflow testing covering charge capture, claim scrubbing, payer adjudication, and remittance posting.
- Built automated ADA Section 508 accessibility testing ensuring patient portal compliance for government healthcare program participants.
- Implemented automated HITECH audit log validation ensuring complete PHI access records for compliance investigations.
- Designed automated ICD-10 and CPT coding accuracy validation for HCA's clinical documentation improvement platform.
- Supported COVID-19 vaccination management system testing enabling rapid deployment across 180 HCA hospitals in 2020-2021.

Environment: Selenium, Java, REST Assured, JMeter, Jenkins, Oracle, SQL Server, HP ALM, Docker


DELOITTE CONSULTING — Houston, TX
Healthcare QA Consultant | Jan 2014–Apr 2016

Healthcare technology QA consultant serving federal health agency and large health system clients.

- Delivered EHR implementation testing services for state Medicaid Management Information Systems (MMIS) clients.
- Conducted HL7 v2 interface testing for state health information exchange (HIE) implementations.
- Performed CMS Meaningful Use Stage 2 certification testing for EHR client deployments.
- Built automated test scripts for state Medicaid eligibility determination and claims adjudication systems.
- Supported federal HITECH program compliance testing for hospital certification submissions.

Environment: Selenium, Java, Postman, SQL Server, Oracle, HP ALM, Jenkins


NOTABLE PROJECTS

Cerner – FHIR Interoperability Certification Test Suite
Comprehensive automated test suite supporting Cerner's ONC 21st Century Cures Act FHIR API certification.

- 500+ automated FHIR API tests covering all ONC required data elements and operations.
- Validated compliance with 8 implementation guides: US Core, PDEX, PDex Plan Net, Da Vinci, Argonaut, SMART, CDS Hooks.
- Zero certification defects identified by ONC reviewers attributable to missed test coverage.
- Test suite shared with 3 other FHIR platform vendors through Cerner's open-source program.

HCA – COVID-19 Vaccine Management Testing Program
Emergency testing program enabling rapid deployment of COVID-19 vaccine scheduling and administration tracking across HCA's 180-hospital network.

- Delivered full regression coverage in 72 hours to support emergency EUA deployment timeline.
- Automated vaccine inventory management, dose scheduling, and CDC VXU reporting workflows.
- Built automated interface testing between vaccine management and state immunization registries.
- System successfully administered 8 million COVID-19 vaccine doses across HCA network.


EDUCATION

M.S. Biomedical Informatics
University of Texas Health Science Center | Houston, TX | 2012–2014
Research on automated clinical decision support validation methods. Thesis: Automated Quality Testing for CDS Rule Engines in EHR Platforms. GPA 3.92/4.0.

B.S. Computer Science
Rice University | Houston, TX | 2008–2012
Magna Cum Laude. Minor in Computational Biology. Rice University Health Informatics research assistant.


CERTIFICATIONS

- CPHIMS – Certified Professional in Health Informatics & Information Management | 2018
- HIPAA Security Officer Certification | 2019
- ISTQB Foundation Level (CTFL) | 2015
- AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner (CLF-C02) | 2022
- Karate DSL API Testing Certification | 2021


PUBLICATIONS, TALKS & COMMUNITY

- 'FHIR API Testing at Scale: Lessons from Cerner's 27,000-Facility Platform' – HL7 International Blog, 2022
- 'Automating HIPAA Compliance Testing' – Healthcare IT News, 2021
- Talk: 'Quality Engineering for 21st Century Cures Act Compliance' – HIMSS22
- Speaker, HIMSS 2022 – HL7 FHIR Testing Automation
- Member, HL7 International Quality Testing Working Group
- Mentor, Women in Health IT Leadership Program

text:
HIROSHI TANAKA
Performance & Reliability Engineer | Gatling | Dynatrace | SRE | Cloud

hiroshi.tanaka.perf@outlook.com | +1 (206) 554-0192 | Seattle, WA | 11+ Years Experience
linkedin.com/in/hiroshitanaka-perf | github.com/htanaka-perf


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Distinguished Performance & Reliability Engineer with 11+ years delivering performance excellence at Amazon, Microsoft Azure, and Fidelity Investments. Expert in Gatling, k6, JMeter, and distributed load generation architectures. Deep APM expertise with Dynatrace PurePath, AppDynamics, New Relic, and custom OpenTelemetry instrumentation. Conducted capacity planning for Amazon Prime Day (600M+ transactions), validated Microsoft Azure service SLAs under global load, and engineered performance test strategies for Fidelity retirement platform serving 40M accounts. Proficient in distributed tracing, JVM profiling (JProfiler, async-profiler), Linux performance analysis (perf, strace, eBPF), and database query optimization across PostgreSQL, Oracle, and Cassandra. Active SRE practitioner aligning performance engineering with error budget management, SLO definition, and automated performance regression gates in CI/CD. Certified Dynatrace Associate and AWS Certified SysOps Administrator.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Performance Tools: Gatling 3.x (Scala/Java), Apache JMeter 5.x (Advanced), k6, Artillery, Locust, BlazeMeter, AWS DLT, LoadRunner Enterprise

APM & Monitoring: Dynatrace (PurePath, Davis AI), AppDynamics, New Relic APM, Datadog APM, Prometheus+Grafana, Jaeger, OpenTelemetry, CloudWatch

Profiling: JProfiler, VisualVM, async-profiler (Flame Graphs), Java Flight Recorder, Chrome DevTools, Android Profiler

Languages: Java 17, Scala 2.13, Python 3.x, Go, JavaScript, PromQL, SQL, Bash

Database Perf: Oracle AWR/ASH, PostgreSQL EXPLAIN, Cassandra nodetool, Redis INFO, MySQL Slow Query

Cloud & Infra: AWS (EC2, ECS, CloudWatch, X-Ray, Aurora), Azure Load Testing, GCP Cloud Trace, Kubernetes HPA

CI/CD: Jenkins, GitHub Actions, k6 Cloud, Grafana k6 Operator, Azure DevOps Pipelines

Chaos: AWS FIS, Gremlin, LitmusChaos, Chaos Toolkit, Chaos Monkey

Networking: CDN Testing (Cloudfront, Akamai), HTTP/2 & HTTP/3, TCP Analysis, Wireshark

SRE: SLO/SLA Definition, Error Budget, Toil Automation, Runbook Automation, Incident Response


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

AMAZON (PRIME & PAYMENTS) — Seattle, WA
Senior Performance Engineer | Jan 2020–Present

Lead performance engineer for Amazon Prime membership, Amazon Pay, and 1-Click checkout platforms supporting the world's largest e-commerce ecosystem.

- Designed and executed Prime Day performance validation simulating 600M+ transactions over 48-hour event window — largest performance test in Amazon retail history.
- Built Gatling Scala performance framework with custom feeders, protocols, and assertions for Amazon Pay checkout API (35K TPS peak SLA).
- Implemented Dynatrace PurePath full-stack instrumentation capturing end-to-end latency breakdowns across Lambda, ECS, and Aurora for all checkout flows.
- Diagnosed critical JVM heap fragmentation in Prime membership service causing GC pauses during high-load periods — resolved by tuning G1GC parameters, eliminating pause storms.
- Built automated performance regression gate in GitHub Actions executing nightly Gatling baseline tests with automated pass/fail vs. prior 30-day SLA median.
- Conducted soak testing for Amazon Subscribe & Save validating 72-hour sustained throughput at 150% expected peak with <2% error rate.
- Implemented OpenTelemetry distributed tracing across 22-hop checkout flow enabling millisecond-precision root cause analysis.
- Built AWS X-Ray service map automation validating latency budget allocations across checkout microservices.
- Designed chaos performance tests using AWS FIS validating checkout behavior under DynamoDB brownout and Lambda cold-start surge scenarios.
- Performed Cassandra cluster performance tuning for Prime membership session store reducing p99 latency from 85ms to 12ms.
- Led post-mortem for Prime Day 2021 traffic incident identifying and resolving root cause CDN cache stampede pattern.
- Built automated capacity forecasting model predicting required ECS task count scaling for holiday events with <8% error margin.
- Implemented real-user monitoring (RUM) validation comparing synthetic test results to CloudWatch RUM production metrics.
- Designed automated performance canary executing scaled-down load tests on every production deployment with rollback trigger.
- Mentored 6 engineers on JVM performance analysis, Gatling scripting, and Dynatrace advanced usage.

Tools: Gatling 3.x (Scala), k6, JMeter, Dynatrace, OpenTelemetry, AWS (ECS, Lambda, DynamoDB, X-Ray, FIS), GitHub Actions, Cassandra, Grafana, async-profiler


MICROSOFT AZURE — Redmond, WA
Performance & Reliability Engineer | Mar 2016–Dec 2019

Performance engineer for Microsoft Azure Storage, Azure Cosmos DB, and Azure Monitor services ensuring globally distributed SLA compliance.

- Built distributed k6 load testing framework executing 10M+ RPS against Azure Blob Storage endpoints validating 99.9% availability SLA under sustained load.
- Implemented automated Azure Cosmos DB performance validation testing RU/s consumption, partition hotspot detection, and cross-region consistency latency.
- Conducted Azure Monitor alerting pipeline performance testing validating sub-60-second metric ingestion and alert trigger latency.
- Built automated CDN edge cache performance testing validating Azure Front Door HIT ratios, TTL behavior, and origin shield effectiveness.
- Designed Python-based automated SLO/SLA compliance reporting comparing actual measured availability to contractual commitments across 6 Azure regions.
- Led performance engineering for Azure Monitor Metrics ingestion pipeline handling 5 billion metrics per day.
- Implemented Azure Load Testing service integration for automated weekly regression baseline testing.

Tools: k6, JMeter, AppDynamics, Azure Load Testing, Python, Prometheus, Grafana, Azure Monitor, Cosmos DB


FIDELITY INVESTMENTS — Boston, MA
QA Performance Analyst | Jul 2013–Feb 2016

Performance QA analyst for Fidelity's retirement account management and trading platforms.

- Executed JMeter load tests for NetBenefits 401(k) platform simulating 1M concurrent participants during open enrollment.
- Built performance baseline documentation for Fidelity brokerage trading system validating market-open spike tolerance.
- Analyzed Oracle AWR reports identifying top SQL statements contributing to query degradation under load.
- Conducted endurance testing for Fidelity Go robo-advisor validating 30-day continuous operation stability.
- Supported StreetScape trading platform performance analysis during high-volatility market events.

Tools: JMeter, HP LoadRunner, Oracle AWR, AppDynamics, SQL Server, Jenkins


NOTABLE PROJECTS

Amazon – Prime Day Performance Engineering Program
Annual engineering program validating Amazon checkout and Prime membership platform readiness for world's largest shopping event.

- 8-week pre-event progressive load test program scaling from 25% to 150% projected peak traffic.
- Identified and resolved 18 performance bottlenecks over 5 Prime Days (2020-2024).
- Validated AWS auto-scaling provisioning 50,000 additional ECS tasks within 90 seconds of demand spike.
- Zero P0 performance incidents during Prime Day events across 5-year program tenure.

Microsoft – Global SLA Validation Framework
Automated framework continuously validating Azure service SLA compliance across 8 global regions.

- Executes 24x7 synthetic load at 5% of peak across all regions detecting regional degradation within 3 minutes.
- Integrated with Azure Monitor alerts and PagerDuty for immediate on-call escalation.
- Automated monthly SLA compliance reports delivered to executive stakeholders.
- Contributed to Azure's 99.99% uptime SLA guarantee credibility with enterprise customers.


EDUCATION

M.S. Computer Science – Systems
University of Washington | Seattle, WA | 2011–2013
Research in distributed system performance modeling. Thesis: Predictive Capacity Planning Using Queue Theory for Microservices. GPA 3.9/4.0.

B.Eng. Information Systems
Keio University | Tokyo, Japan | 2007–2011
First Class Honours. Exchange scholar at MIT CSAIL. Software Engineering Club president.


CERTIFICATIONS

- Dynatrace Associate Performance Engineer | 2021
- AWS Certified SysOps Administrator – Associate (SOA-C02) | 2021
- Gatling Expert Certification | 2021
- ISTQB Performance Testing Specialist (CT-PT) | 2018
- Google Cloud Professional DevOps Engineer | 2022


PUBLICATIONS & PROFESSIONAL COMMUNITY

- 'Diagnosing JVM GC Pressure Under Prime Day Load' – AWS re:Invent Blog, 2022
- 'k6 vs Gatling: Enterprise Performance Tool Selection Guide 2023' – InfoQ
- Talk: 'Zero P0 Prime Days: 5 Years of Performance Engineering at Amazon' – Velocity Conf 2023
- Speaker, Velocity Conference 2023 – Amazon Prime Day Performance Engineering
- Dynatrace ACE (Automation & Configuration Expert) Community Member
- Open Source: gatling-aws-plugin library (GitHub 780+ stars)

text:
KEISHA RENEE THOMPSON
SDET | React Native | Node.js | GraphQL | Retail Tech

keisha.thompson.sdet@protonmail.com | +1 (404) 771-2293 | Atlanta, GA | 9+ Years Experience


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

Innovative SDET with 9+ years of specialized experience testing React, React Native, Node.js, and GraphQL applications at The Home Depot, NCR Corporation, and Accenture. Expert in Cypress, Playwright, Jest, React Testing Library, and Apollo GraphQL testing with a strong emphasis on component-level quality and design system validation. Built comprehensive accessibility testing programs (WCAG 2.1 AA) and visual regression pipelines using Percy and Chromatic at enterprise scale. Proven ability to reduce front-end defect escape rates by 51% through component contract testing and automated visual diff validation. Strong background in retail omnichannel testing including BOPIS, curbside pickup, inventory management APIs, and loyalty program integration. AWS Certified Developer and IAAP CPWA accessibility certification holder. Active conference speaker on modern JavaScript testing techniques.


CORE TECHNICAL COMPETENCIES

Testing Frameworks: Cypress 13+, Playwright 1.40+, Jest 29+, Vitest, React Testing Library, Enzyme (legacy)

Component & Visual: Storybook Testing, Chromatic, Percy, BackstopJS, Loki, Component Story Format 3

API Testing: Apollo GraphQL Testing, Supertest, MSW (Mock Service Worker), REST APIs, WebSocket Testing

Languages: TypeScript 5.x, JavaScript (ES2022), Python, SQL, CSS/SCSS

Mobile: React Native Testing (Detox 20+), Maestro, Appium (WDIO)

CI/CD: GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Vercel CI, Netlify, Turborepo, Docker

Cloud: AWS (Amplify, CloudFront, Lambda, DynamoDB, S3), Firebase

Accessibility: axe-core, WAVE, Deque aXe DevTools, VoiceOver/NVDA automation, ARIA testing

Performance: Lighthouse CI, Web Vitals (CWV), k6, Puppeteer CDP

State & Data: Redux Testing, React Query Testing, Zustand, Apollo Cache validation


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

THE HOME DEPOT — Atlanta, GA
Senior SDET – Digital Commerce | Feb 2021–Present

Senior test engineer for The Home Depot's omnichannel retail platform serving 40M+ monthly online shoppers across THD.com, Pro Xtra B2B portal, and Home Depot mobile apps.

- Architected Playwright TypeScript automation framework for The Home Depot website covering product search, PDP, cart, checkout, order management, and account self-service flows with 1,200+ test scenarios.
- Designed React Testing Library + Jest component test strategy for The Home Depot's Design System achieving 96% coverage across 180+ shared UI components.
- Built Storybook + Chromatic visual regression pipeline detecting pixel-level defects in Design System components, catching 240+ visual regressions in 2-year period.
- Implemented automated WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility pipeline using axe-core enforcing zero-tolerance for critical violations across all customer-facing pages.
- Built Apollo GraphQL API testing framework validating product catalog, pricing, inventory, and personalization queries with schema validation using graphql-schema-linter.
- Designed Detox automation for The Home Depot iOS/Android apps covering in-store mode, pro purchase, and product scanning features.
- Implemented Lighthouse CI performance budget enforcement preventing Core Web Vitals regressions (LCP, INP, CLS) in production deploys.
- Built BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick Up In-Store) end-to-end automation validating order placement through store fulfillment and curbside handoff.
- Implemented automated Pro Xtra loyalty point calculation and redemption validation across 500K+ professional contractor accounts.
- Designed automated A/B experiment validation for 30+ concurrent conversion rate optimization tests on search and PDP pages.
- Reduced front-end production defect escape rate by 51% through component contract testing and visual regression automation.
- Built automated cross-locale testing for US, Canada (English/French), and Mexico storefronts.
- Implemented automated ADA Section 508 compliance testing for B2B Pro Xtra portal used by government contractors.
- Established PR-level component test gate preventing UI merges without 80% component coverage threshold.
- Mentored 5 junior SDETs on TypeScript testing patterns, Playwright best practices, and accessibility testing.

Environment: Playwright, TypeScript, React Testing Library, Jest, Chromatic, Detox, Apollo GraphQL, axe-core, Lighthouse CI, GitHub Actions, AWS Amplify, Storybook


NCR CORPORATION — Atlanta, GA
QA Automation Engineer – POS & Payments | Jun 2017–Jan 2021

Automation engineer for NCR's Aloha POS, Silver commerce, and payment processing platforms serving 100,000+ restaurants and retailers.

- Built Selenium WebDriver automation suite for NCR Aloha POS back-office web interface covering menu management, reporting, and configuration workflows.
- Developed API automation for NCR payment processing APIs covering card-present, card-not-present, EMV chip, and contactless NFC transactions.
- Implemented automated POS terminal integration testing validating Verifone, Ingenico, and PAX device communication protocols.
- Designed performance testing for NCR's cloud POS platform validating sub-200ms transaction approval under 50,000 concurrent transactions.
- Built automated EMV L3 test suite validating chip card application selection, transaction flow, and cryptogram verification.
- Automated tip adjustment and void transaction workflow testing for full-service restaurant scenarios.
- Integrated test automation with Jenkins for continuous regression on NCR Silver cloud POS deployments.
- Implemented automated inventory depletion and 86-item validation for restaurant menu management.

Environment: Selenium, Java, REST Assured, JMeter, Jenkins, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Postman


ACCENTURE — Atlanta, GA
Test Engineer – Retail & CPG | Aug 2015–May 2017

Test engineer within Accenture's Retail & Consumer Goods practice serving major CPG and retail clients.

- Executed functional and regression testing for SAP Hybris e-commerce implementations covering product catalog, cart, and checkout.
- Built Selenium automation scripts for retail client portals and B2B order management systems.
- Performed API testing for product information management (PIM) system integrations.
- Supported SAP ERP integration testing for order-to-cash and procure-to-pay processes.
- Conducted database validation for product data migration between legacy and new PIM systems.

Environment: Selenium, Java, Postman, SAP Hybris, Oracle, Jenkins, JIRA


NOTABLE PROJECTS

Home Depot – Accessible Design System Testing Program
Enterprise accessibility automation ensuring The Home Depot's shared component library meets WCAG 2.1 AA for all consuming teams.

- axe-core scanning 180+ components on every Storybook publish with zero-tolerance enforcement.
- Custom Playwright scripts automating screen reader navigation for 40 critical component interaction patterns.
- Established accessibility regression baseline preventing compliant components from future violations.
- Enabled THD government contractor portals to achieve ADA Section 508 compliance for federal business.

Home Depot – Omnichannel Automation Suite
End-to-end automation covering seamless BOPIS, curbside, same-day delivery, and ship-from-store journeys.

- Single test suite validating order journey across web, mobile app, and store associate app.
- Automated real-time inventory sync validation across 2,300 US store locations.
- Built automated carrier API testing for UPS, FedEx, and USPS shipping label generation.
- Zero critical BOPIS defects post-automation deployment across 4 major Black Friday/holiday seasons.


EDUCATION

M.S. Human-Computer Interaction
Georgia Institute of Technology | Atlanta, GA | 2013–2015
Research on automated accessibility evaluation tools for dynamic SPA applications. GPA 3.85/4.0. HCI Lab research assistant.

B.S. Computer Science
Spelman College | Atlanta, GA | 2009–2013
Summa Cum Laude. First in class. Google BOLD Intern. Winner of Anita Borg Institute scholarship.


CERTIFICATIONS

- IAAP Certified Professional in Web Accessibility (CPWA) | 2021
- AWS Certified Developer – Associate (DVA-C02) | 2022
- Playwright Advanced Testing Certification | 2022
- Cypress.io Certified Engineer | 2020
- ISTQB Agile Testing Certification (CT-ATE) | 2019


PUBLICATIONS & TALKS

- 'Accessibility Automation at The Home Depot: WCAG 2.1 at Scale' – A11y Weekly, 2022
- 'Visual Regression Testing: Chromatic vs Percy vs BackstopJS' – Smashing Magazine, 2021
- Talk: 'Testing React Design Systems' – React Summit Amsterdam 2023

ELARA V. STERLING, LEED AP, PMP

Vice President of Global Sustainability & Renewable Energy Infrastructure

Location: 1402 Horizon Terrace, Denver, CO 80202 Phone: (303) 555-0821 Email: e.sterling.esg@vanguard-renewables.com LinkedIn: https://www.google.com/search?q=linkedin.com/in/elara-sterling-sustainability-exec

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

A pioneering and high-impact Sustainability Executive with over 12 years of executive-level experience in spearheading multi-billion dollar renewable energy transitions and global ESG strategies for industrial conglomerates and utility-scale energy providers. Proven expertise in the lifecycle management of utility-scale solar, onshore/offshore wind, and battery energy storage systems (BESS), with a career track record of delivering over 12GW of clean energy capacity to global grids. Strategic leader who has managed departmental budgets exceeding $250M and directed cross-functional, multi-continental teams of 300+ engineers, environmental scientists, and policy experts. Renowned for architecting "Net-Zero" roadmaps that align corporate profitability with aggressive decarbonization targets, resulting in an average carbon footprint reduction of 35% across diverse asset portfolios. Adept at navigating complex international regulatory landscapes, including EU Taxonomy, SEC Climate Disclosures, and TCFD frameworks. A trusted advisor to C-suite executives and boards of directors, focused on transforming sustainability from a compliance requirement into a primary driver of competitive advantage and shareholder value.

CORE COMPETENCIES

	Renewable Energy Strategy: Utility-scale Solar (PV), Wind (Onshore/Offshore), Green Hydrogen, and Geothermal development.
	ESG Frameworks & Reporting: Expert mastery of GRI, SASB, TCFD, CDP, and Integrated Reporting (IR) standards.
	Capital Project Management: Oversight of $1.5B+ infrastructure projects, including EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) management.
	Decarbonization & Net-Zero: Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions accounting, Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) alignment.
	Grid Modernization: Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), Microgrid development, and Smart Grid integration.
	Policy & Regulatory Affairs: Mastery of Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) rulings, Renewable Portfolio Standards (RPS), and the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).
	Sustainable Finance: Green Bond issuance, Carbon Credit markets, and Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) negotiations.
	Environmental Compliance: NEPA/CEQA environmental impact assessments, biodiversity preservation, and waste-to-energy circularity.
	Stakeholder Engagement: Building coalitions with indigenous communities, governmental bodies, and institutional investors (e.g., BlackRock, Vanguard).
	Operational Excellence: Lean Six Sigma application to renewable asset O&M (Operations & Maintenance).

TECHNICAL SKILLS

	Energy Modeling & Simulation: PVSyst, WindPRO, HOMER Pro, SAM (System Advisor Model).
	Sustainability Software: Enablon, Metrio (Sustainalytics), Persefoni (Carbon Accounting), Watershed.
	Project Governance: Primavera P6, Microsoft Project, Procore (Construction Management), Smartsheet.
	GIS & Spatial Analysis: ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, Google Earth Engine for environmental site assessment.
	Data Science & Analytics: Python (NumPy/Pandas for energy load forecasting), Tableau for ESG dashboards, SQL.
	Standards & Certifications: LEED BD+C, WELL Building Standard, ISO 14001 (EMS), ISO 50001 (Energy Management).

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

VANGUARD RENEWABLES GROUP | DENVER, CO

Vice President of Global Sustainability & Infrastructure Strategy | January 2020 – Present

Leads the global sustainability vision and renewable infrastructure portfolio for a $9B energy firm. Responsible for the transition of legacy fossil fuel assets to high-efficiency renewable energy systems across 14 countries.
Renewable Portfolio Growth: Directed the expansion of the utility-scale solar and wind portfolio from 3.5GW to 12.2GW in 48 months, contributing to a 40% increase in company valuation.
Decarbonization Leadership: Authored and implemented the "Vanguard 2035 Net-Zero Blueprint," achieving a verified 22% reduction in Scope 1 and 2 emissions within the first three years.
PPA Negotiation: Successfully negotiated 15 long-term Virtual Power Purchase Agreements (VPPAs) with Fortune 100 clients (including TechGiant Corp and Global Retail Ltd), securing $4.5B in guaranteed long-term revenue.
Green Bond Issuance: Orchestrated the company’s inaugural $750M Green Bond offering, which was 4x oversubscribed, to fund the development of the "Aeolus" offshore wind project.
Supply Chain Circularity: Launched a global solar panel recycling program, ensuring that 95% of decommissioned assets are diverted from landfills and reclaimed for raw materials.
Strategic M&A: Led environmental and technical due diligence for the $1.2B acquisition of Summit Wind Systems, integrating 850MW of active assets into the Vanguard grid within 6 months.
Policy Advocacy: Represented the organization at the United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP26/27), influencing international policy on carbon pricing and renewable energy subsidies.
Executive Mentorship: Established the "Sustainability Leaders Program," training 50+ mid-level managers in ESG integration, resulting in 12 internal promotions to Director level.

MERIDIAN ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE | HOUSTON, TX

Director of Renewable Development – North America | June 2016 – December 2019

Managed the North American development pipeline for renewable energy projects, focusing on site acquisition, permitting, and grid interconnection.
Interconnection Excellence: Successfully navigated the MISO and PJM interconnection queues for 12 separate solar-plus-storage projects, reducing average wait times by 15% through strategic technical positioning.
BESS Deployment: Led the design and deployment of one of the world's largest Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Texas (250MW/1000MWh), providing critical grid stability during peak demand periods.
Community Relations: Developed a "Community Benefit Framework" that secured unanimous local government approval for the Prairie Sun project, despite initial NIMBY (Not In My Backyard) opposition.
Cost Optimization: Implemented a new EPC procurement strategy that reduced the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) for new wind projects by 12% through bulk purchasing and strategic vendor diversification.
Environmental Mitigation: Designed a first-of-its-kind avian protection system for wind turbines using AI-enabled camera detection, reducing bird strike incidents by 85% and ensuring NEPA compliance.
Digital Transformation: Integrated ArcGIS-based predictive modeling to identify high-yield solar sites, increasing the success rate of land options by 30%.
Team Leadership: Directed a multi-disciplinary team of 85, including land agents, electrical engineers, and regulatory attorneys, achieving a 98% project completion rate against original timelines.

HORIZON SUSTAINABILITY CONSULTING | SEATTLE, WA

Senior Manager, ESG Strategy & Climate Risk | March 2012 – May 2016

Provided strategic advisory services to multinational corporations in the manufacturing, technology, and mining sectors.
Climate Risk Assessment: Conducted detailed TCFD-aligned climate scenario analyses for Alpha Mining Group (fictional client), identifying over $200M in potential stranded assets and outlining a 10-year divestment strategy.
Reporting Transformation: Re-engineered the CSR reporting process for Pacific Tech Solutions, transitioning them from basic narrative reports to data-driven, SASB-compliant ESG disclosures that improved their Sustainalytics rating from "High Risk" to "Low Risk."
Supply Chain Audit: Led a global Scope 3 emissions audit of 500+ suppliers across Southeast Asia, uncovering 15,000 metric tons of unaccounted-for carbon and establishing a supplier code of conduct.
Energy Efficiency Programs: Designed a portfolio-wide energy retrofitting program for a commercial real estate firm, resulting in a 25% reduction in annual utility spend across 50 properties.
Certification Success: Guided 12 major commercial developments through the LEED Gold and Platinum certification process, enhancing asset value and tenant retention.

PACIFIC NORTHWEST RENEWABLES | PORTLAND, OR

Senior Project Engineer / Energy Analyst | January 2008 – February 2012

Technical lead for the feasibility study and engineering design of regional renewable energy projects.
Resource Assessment: Conducted over 50 wind resource assessments and solar irradiance studies, utilizing PVSyst and WindPRO to generate high-accuracy production forecasts for project financing.
Microgrid Design: Designed and oversaw the construction of a hybrid wind-solar-diesel microgrid for a remote island community, reducing their fuel dependency by 70%.
Performance Monitoring: Developed a real-time SCADA dashboard to monitor the performance of 500+ distributed solar installations, improving maintenance response times by 40%.
Technical Writing: Authored technical specifications for EPC contracts, ensuring all installations met IEEE and NERC reliability standards.

KEY PROJECTS PORTFOLIO

Project Aeolus: 1.5GW Offshore Wind Development
Objective: To develop and commission the largest offshore wind farm in the Atlantic region.
Role: Lead Project Director and Infrastructure Strategist.
Action: Managed the $3.2B project lifecycle, from ocean floor leasing and environmental impact statements (EIS) to the procurement of 150 Siemens Gamesa turbines. Orchestrated the complex logistics of specialized vessel chartering and subsea cable installation.
Outcome: Successfully reached "Commercial Operation Date" (COD) on schedule. Currently powers over 600,000 homes with 100% renewable energy.
KPIs: 99.8% safety record over 5 million man-hours; 18% IRR (Internal Rate of Return) for investors.
Project Terra: The Global Supply Chain Decarbonization Initiative
Objective: To eliminate Scope 3 emissions across the Vanguard Renewables Tier 1 supply chain.
Role: ESG Executive Sponsor.
Action: Deployed a blockchain-based tracking platform to verify the "Green Credentials" of steel and silicon suppliers. Implemented a "Carbon Tax" on suppliers failing to meet 5% annual reduction targets.
Outcome: Reduced total value-chain emissions by 1.2 million metric tons of CO2e in 24 months. Received the "Sustainable Supply Chain Excellence" award from the UN.
KPIs: 92% supplier participation rate; 100% audit transparency.
Project Helios: Utility-Scale Solar + 4-Hour BESS Integration
Objective: To solve the "Duck Curve" energy challenge in the Southwest US.
Role: Technical Implementation Lead.
Action: Integrated a 500MW solar field with a 200MW/800MWh Tesla Megapack system. Developed a custom AI dispatch algorithm that sells energy during peak pricing windows (4 PM - 8 PM).
Outcome: Increased project revenue by 25% compared to standalone solar. Provided the regional grid with essential frequency regulation and spinning reserves.
KPIs: 95% round-trip efficiency for BESS; zero curtailment during peak solar production.
Project Catalyst: Sustainable Hydrogen Pilot
Objective: To test the viability of "
Green Hydrogen" for heavy industrial heating.
Role: Innovation & Research Director.
Action: Installed a 20MW PEM electrolyzer powered by on-site wind energy at a partner steel mill (IronRidge Steel). Developed the safety and transportation protocols for high-pressure hydrogen storage.
Outcome: Proved that green hydrogen can replace 30% of natural gas in blast furnaces without compromising steel quality.
KPIs: $4/kg production cost (projected); 99% purity of hydrogen output.

LEADERSHIP & TEAM MANAGEMENT

Strategic Org Design: Restructured the Sustainability department at Vanguard into three core pillars: Strategy & Reporting, Infrastructure Development, and Operational O&M. This improved project throughput by 30%.
Global Talent Acquisition: Personally led the recruitment of 40+ specialized environmental engineers and data scientists to build the "Vanguard Center for Climate Analytics."
Inclusive Leadership: Championed the "Renewables for All" initiative, ensuring that 20% of Vanguard’s new projects are located in or provide direct financial benefit to economically disadvantaged communities.
Crisis Leadership: Successfully maintained project continuity during the 2021 global logistics crisis by establishing a "Strategic Inventory Reserve" for critical components (inverters, transformers), preventing over $150M in delay-related penalties.

PROCESS IMPROVEMENTS & AUTOMATION ACHIEVEMENTS

Automated ESG Data Aggregator: Developed an API-driven tool that pulls real-time energy and water consumption data from 200+ global facilities into a central "Single Source of Truth" dashboard, reducing manual reporting hours by 12,000 per year.
AI-Enabled Site Scouting: Implemented a machine-learning algorithm that analyzes satellite imagery and topographic data to predict the "Solar Potential Index" of land parcels, reducing the site assessment phase from 3 months to 2 days.
Predictive Maintenance (PdM) for Wind: Deployed vibration and thermal sensors across the global wind fleet. Utilized a "Digital Twin" model to predict gearbox failures 6 weeks in advance, reducing unplanned downtime by 35% and saving $12M in annual repair costs.
Permitting Workflow Automation: Created a digital "Regulatory Tracker" that uses NLP to monitor changes in local zoning laws and environmental regulations, automatically alerting project managers to potential compliance risks.

CERTIFICATIONS

	LEED Accredited Professional (BD+C) – U.S. Green Building Council
	PMP (Project Management Professional) – Project Management Institute
	Sustainability and Climate Risk (SCR®) Certificate – GARP
	Certified Energy Manager (CEM) – Association of Energy Engineers
	Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) Certified Professional
	Six Sigma Black Belt (Focus on Industrial Sustainability)

EDUCATION

Master of Science in Sustainable Energy Systems Stanford University | Stanford, CA Focus: Grid Integration of Variable Renewable Energy; Thesis on "The Economics of Long-Duration Energy Storage"
Bachelor of Science in Environmental Engineering University of Colorado Boulder | Boulder, CO Graduated Magna Cum Laude; President of the Renewable Energy Society

TOOLS & TECHNOLOGIES

	Analysis: PVSyst, WindPRO, RetScreen Expert, HOMER Pro, SAM.
	ESG Platforms: Persefoni, Watershed, Enablon, Metrio.
	Engineering: AutoCAD (Electrical), Revit (BIM), ArcGIS Pro.
	Project Ops: Procore, Primavera P6, Oracle NetSuite (ERP Integration).
	Data/Cloud: AWS (S3/Lambda for data processing), Tableau, Microsoft Power Platform.

PUBLICATIONS & CONFERENCES

	Keynote Speaker: "Decarbonizing the Heavy Industry: The Role of Green Hydrogen," World Energy Congress 2024, Rotterdam, NL.
	Author: "The 24/7 Clean Energy Goal: Moving Beyond Annual Offsets," Harvard Business Review – Sustainability Edition, Q3 2023.
	Contributor: "Guidelines for the Responsible Sourcing of Solar Minerals," International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) White Paper.
	Guest Lecturer: "Financing the Transition: Green Bonds and the Future of Energy Infrastructure," Columbia University – Center on Global Energy Policy.

PROFESSIONAL AFFILIATIONS

	Executive Board Member, American Clean Power Association (ACP)
	Member, Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)
	Fellow, Institute of Environmental Management and Assessment (IEMA)
	Advisory Board Member, Rocky Mountain Institute (RMI)

PERSONAL DETAILS

	Clearance: Public Trust (inactive).
	Travel: Fully open to international travel (60%+).
	Languages: English (Native), Spanish (Full Professional Proficiency), Portuguese (Working Knowledge).
	Interests: Regenerative Agriculture, Alpine Skiing, Wilderness Conservation.

Pavan Kumar
https://www.linkedin.com/in/pavan-kumar-10rad/ | +1(859) 567-9177 | pavan03248@gmail.com


Job Description :
The Flow Engine platform consists of a UI and a list of Java backend services, including a Flow Authoring Tool and a Flow Execution Engine, enabling users to create, edit, publish, and execute workflows that drive user experiences and business processes.
 
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and maintain the Flow Engine platform, focusing on both the Flow Authoring Tool and the Flow Execution Engine.
• Design and implement features that support drag-and-drop functionality for creating and linking process elements on a canvas.
• Collaborate with multiple flow authoring teams to create and run step/task-based UI experiences across various customer and agent channels.
• Optimize system performance and ensure scalability and reliability.
• Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to the Flow Engine platform.
• Architect system solutions and think outside the box to solve complex problems.
 
Required Skills and Qualifications:
• Front-End Development: Proficiency in Angular, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
• Back-End Development: Strong experience with Java and Spring Boot microservices.
• No Code / Low Code Platforms: Experience with developing or using no code/low code platforms.
• UI/UX Design: Experience with creating intuitive and user-friendly interfaces, particularly with drag-and-drop functionality.
• Data Processing: Knowledge of batch processing, streaming, and real-time data processing techniques.
• DevOps: Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker), and orchestration tools (Kubernetes).
• Core Java: Proficiency in core Java concepts and practices.
• Multithreading: Experience with multithreading and concurrent programming.
• Queues: Knowledge of message queuing systems.
• Jolt: Experience with Jolt for JSON transformations.
• Couchbase: Familiarity with Couchbase or similar NoSQL databases.
• Problem-Solving: Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills.
• Collaboration: Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
• Architectural Thinking: Ability to architect system solutions and think creatively to solve complex problems.
 
Preferred Qualifications:
• Experience with policy orchestration and version management.
• Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
• Knowledge of database technologies (SQL, NoSQL).
• Understanding of data security and compliance standards.
 
Additional Skills:
o Experience with microservices architecture and design patterns.
o Knowledge of API design and development.
o Familiarity with Agile and Scrum methodologies.
o Experience with performance tuning and optimization.
o Understanding of event-driven architecture.
o Familiarity with monitoring and logging tools (e.g., ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana).
 


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
•	Developed and maintained a sophisticated Flow Engine platform, encompassing both the Flow Authoring Tool and Flow Execution Engine. Continuously developed new features and optimized existing functionality to enhance the platform's capabilities and user experience.
•	Implemented advanced drag-and-drop functionality for the Flow Authoring Tool, enabling intuitive creation and linking of process elements on a canvas. Refined the drag-and-drop interface to improve usability and efficiency for workflow designers.
•	Utilized Angular, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create responsive and interactive front-end components for the Flow Engine platform. Leveraged Angular's powerful features to build reusable UI components and optimize application performance.
•	Designed and developed robust Java and Spring Boot microservices to support the backend operations of the Flow Engine. Implemented scalable Java-based solutions to handle complex workflow processing and data management requirements.
•	Applied expertise in no code / low code platforms to enhance the Flow Engine's capabilities, allowing users to create sophisticated workflows with minimal coding. Integrated no code / low code features to streamline the workflow creation process for non-technical users.
•	Collaborated with multiple flow authoring teams to create and execute step/task-based UI experiences across various customer and agent channels. Ensured seamless integration of workflows across different platforms and user interfaces.
•	Optimized system performance through advanced multithreading and concurrent programming techniques. Implemented efficient multithreading solutions to improve the Flow Execution Engine's processing capabilities and responsiveness.
•	Designed and implemented scalable message queuing systems to facilitate asynchronous communication between microservices. Utilized queues to improve system reliability and handle high-volume data processing requirements.
•	Leveraged Jolt for complex JSON transformations, enabling flexible data mapping and integration within the Flow Engine. Developed custom Jolt specifications to handle diverse data structures and ensure accurate information flow.
•	Implemented Couchbase as the primary NoSQL database for the Flow Engine platform, optimizing data storage and retrieval operations. Designed efficient Couchbase queries and indexing strategies to support high-performance workflow execution.
•	Architected system solutions to address complex problems, applying creative thinking and innovative approaches. Developed comprehensive architectural designs to ensure scalability, maintainability, and extensibility of the Flow Engine platform.
•	Implemented robust CI/CD pipelines using containerization (Docker) and orchestration tools (Kubernetes) to streamline development and deployment processes. Optimized DevOps practices to increase release frequency and improve overall system reliability.
•	Applied expertise in batch processing, streaming, and real-time data processing techniques to handle diverse data requirements within the Flow Engine. Implemented efficient data processing algorithms to support high-volume workflow execution.
•	Demonstrated strong analytical and troubleshooting skills by identifying and resolving complex technical issues related to the Flow Engine platform. Developed systematic approaches to diagnose and address performance bottlenecks and functional discrepancies.
•	Contributed to the design and development of APIs for seamless integration with external systems and services. Implemented RESTful APIs following best practices to ensure security, scalability, and ease of consumption by third-party applications.
•	Utilized knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) to design and implement cloud-native solutions for the Flow Engine. Leveraged cloud services to enhance scalability, reliability, and cost-effectiveness of the platform.
•	Implemented robust data security measures and ensured compliance with industry standards throughout the Flow Engine platform. Applied best practices in data security to protect sensitive information and maintain regulatory compliance.
•	Employed Agile and Scrum methodologies to manage development cycles and ensure timely delivery of new features and improvements. Participated in sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives to maintain efficient Agile development processes.


TECHNICAL SKILLS

Programming Languages: Java (8,11,17 & 21), C/C++, Python.
Java Technologies: Core Java, J2EE, JSP, Servlets, Spring MVC, Spring Boot, Hibernate, RESTful API, SOAP.
Web Technologies: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, jQuery, TypeScript, Node.JS, Angular (2,4,14, & 17), React (16.3,16.8,17), Redux, AJAX, Tailwind CSS, Webpack, Babel, Vue.js.
Spring Framework: Spring (IOC, AOP, DAO, Batch, Cloud, Security).
Web/Application Servers: IBM WebSphere, WebLogic, Apache Tomcat, JBOSS.
Web Services: Swagger, Open API, RESTful API, GraphQL, GRPC, Apigee API, Camunda, Mule Soft, SOAP.
Testing & Automation Tools: Cypress, Selenium, Postman, JUnit, Mockito, Jest, JMeter, Mocha, Karma, Cucumber.
Development Methodologies: Agile (Safe 6.x), TDD, BDD, EDD, Scrum, Kanban.
IDE: My Eclipse, IntelliJ Idea, Microsoft Visual Studio.
Databases: MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle11g/10g/9i, SQL Server, Cassandra, DynamoDB, MongoDB, DB2, Redis.
Cloud Platforms: AWS, AZURE, GCP.
Version Control: Git, Bitbucket, SVN, CVS.
Build Tools: Maven, Gradle, Ant.
Other Tools: Log4J, Jenkins, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform.
Monitoring: Grafana, Prometheus, ELK Stack, Splunk.
Messaging Queue: Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, IBM MQ.
Operating Systems: Windows, UNIX, Linux.

CERTIFICATIONS 
AWS Certified Developer - Associate
Azure Certified Developer – Associate
Oracle Gen AI Certified 

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Bank of America   North Carolina                                                                                                                          
 Sr. Full Stack Developer	                                                                                      July 2021 - Present  				                                                                
Environment: Java, Angular, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Spring Boot, No Code / Low Code Platforms, Docker, Kubernetes, Core Java, Multithreading, Message Queuing Systems, Jolt, Couchbase, AWS, Azure, GCP, SQL, NoSQL, Microservices, API, Agile, Scrum, ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, CI/CD pipelines
•	Designed and implemented a robust microservices architecture using Java and Spring Boot for a core banking system, enhancing scalability and maintainability. Utilized Docker and Kubernetes for containerization and orchestration, resulting in improved deployment efficiency and system reliability.
•	Developed responsive and interactive user interfaces using Angular and HTML/CSS/JavaScript, integrating real-time data visualization for banking dashboards. Implemented Angular material design components to ensure a consistent and user-friendly experience across various banking applications.
•	Engineered high-performance RESTful APIs using Java and Spring Boot, facilitating seamless integration between front-end and back-end systems. Implemented OAuth 2.0 authentication and JWT tokens to enhance API security, ensuring secure data transmission in banking transactions.
•	Optimized SQL and NoSQL database queries, significantly improving data retrieval times for critical banking operations. Utilized Couchbase for efficient document storage and retrieval, implementing advanced indexing strategies to enhance query performance in NoSQL environments.
•	Implemented comprehensive logging and monitoring solutions using the ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) for real-time system analysis. Integrated Prometheus and Grafana for advanced metrics collection and visualization, enabling proactive identification of potential issues in banking applications.
•	Developed and maintained CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitLab CI, automating build, test, and deployment processes for banking applications. Utilized Docker images and Kubernetes deployments to ensure consistent and reliable application delivery across multiple environments.
•	Implemented multithreading techniques in Core Java to optimize performance-critical banking operations, such as transaction processing and report generation. Utilized Java concurrency utilities to manage thread pools and ensure thread safety in high-load scenarios.
•	Designed and implemented a microservices-based fraud detection system using Spring Boot and machine learning algorithms. Integrated real-time data processing capabilities using Apache Kafka to enable immediate fraud alerts and prevention measures.
•	Utilized cloud technologies including AWS and Azureto deploy and scale banking applications, ensuring high availability and disaster recovery. Implemented cloud-native services such as managed databases and serverless functions to optimize resource utilization and cost-efficiency.
•	Developed a custom ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline using Java and Apache Spark for processing large volumes of financial data. Implemented Jolt transformations to standardize data formats and ensure data quality across various banking systems.
•	Implemented security best practices throughout the application stack, including input validation, encryption of sensitive data, and secure communication protocols. Conducted regular security audits and penetration testing to identify and address potential vulnerabilities in banking applications.
•	Optimized front-end performance using advanced JavaScript techniques, including lazy loading and code splitting. Implemented Progressive Web App (PWA) features to enhance the mobile banking experience, improving offline capabilities and reducing load times.
•	Designed and implemented a distributed caching solution using Redis to improve response times for frequently accessed banking data. Integrated Couchbase as a persistent data store, leveraging its built-in caching mechanisms to further enhance system performance.
•	Developed custom Angular directives and services to create reusable UI components for various banking applications. Implemented RxJS observables and operators to manage complex asynchronous operations in Angular, improving overall application responsiveness.
•	Utilized Agile methodologies and Scrum practices to manage development cycles and ensure timely delivery of banking features. Conducted regular code reviews and pair programming sessions to maintain code quality and share knowledge within the development team.


Starbucks	                                                     		                                                                California                  
Sr. Java Full Stack Developer					                               Jan 2020 to June 2021

Environment: Java, Angular, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Spring Boot, No Code / Low Code Platforms, Docker, Kubernetes, Core Java, Multithreading, Message Queuing Systems, Jolt, Couchbase, AWS, Azure, GCP, SQL, NoSQL, Microservices, API, Agile, Scrum, ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, CI/CD pipelines
•	Designed and implemented a robust microservices architecture using Java 11 and Spring Boot 2.3, enabling seamless integration of Starbucks' order management system. Utilized Docker containers for efficient deployment and Kubernetes for orchestration, ensuring high availability and scalability of services.
•	Developed responsive and interactive user interfaces for Starbucks' mobile app using Angular 9 and TypeScript, implementing Core Java concepts for business logic. Integrated RESTful APIs to facilitate real-time communication between front-end and back-end services, enhancing overall user experience.
•	Engineered and optimized complex SQL queries for Oracle Database 19c, improving data retrieval efficiency for Starbucks' inventory management system. Implemented NoSQL solutions using Couchbase for handling unstructured data, resulting in faster read/write operations for customer preferences.
•	Implemented multithreading techniques in Java to enhance the performance of Starbucks' order processing system, reducing response times during peak hours. Utilized Java concurrency frameworks to manage thread pools and ensure thread safety in critical sections of the application.
•	Designed and developed RESTful APIs using Spring Boot and Java, enabling seamless integration between Starbucks' mobile app and back-end services. Implemented OAuth 2.0 authentication and JWT tokens to ensure secure communication between microservices and client applications.
•	Utilized ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) for centralized logging and monitoring of Starbucks' distributed systems. Implemented Prometheus and Grafana dashboards to visualize key performance metrics and set up alerts for proactive issue resolution.
•	Migrated Starbucks' legacy monolithic application to a microservices architecture, leveraging Docker containers and Kubernetes for efficient deployment and scaling. Implemented Circuit Breaker patterns using Resilience4j to enhance fault tolerance and system reliability.
•	Developed and maintained CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitLab CI, automating build, test, and deployment processes for Starbucks' Java applications. Implemented Docker image scanning and vulnerability assessments to ensure security compliance throughout the development lifecycle.
•	Optimized Starbucks' loyalty program backend using Java 11 streams and lambda expressions, improving data processing efficiency. Implemented caching mechanisms with Redis to reduce database load and enhance response times for frequently accessed customer data.
•	Designed and implemented a real-time analytics system using Apache Kafka and Spring Boot, enabling Starbucks to process and analyze customer ordering patterns. Utilized AWS Lambda functions for serverless event-driven processing of streaming data.

Full Stack Java Developer							                                  Feb 2018 to Dec 2019
Environment: Java, Angular, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Spring Boot, No Code / Low Code Platforms, Docker, Kubernetes, Core Java, Multithreading, Message Queuing Systems, Jolt, Couchbase, AWS, Azure, GCP, SQL, NoSQL, Microservices, API, Agile, Scrum, ELK stack, Prometheus, Grafana, CI/CD pipelines
•	Developed Angular components for financial dashboard interfaces, enhancing Angular performance through lazy loading and efficient change detection strategies.
•	Created responsive React applications with Redux state management, implementing optimized React rendering techniques to handle real-time financial data display.

•	Enhanced UX for credit monitoring applications through iterative design, applying UX methodologies to simplify complex financial information presentation for users.
•	Improved SEO rankings through technical optimizations and content strategies, implementing SEO best practices to increase organic visibility for credit product pages.
•	Conducted A/B Testing for signup flow optimizations across platforms, analyzing A/B Testing metrics to identify interface improvements for conversion enhancement.
•	Customized WordPress installation for company blog and knowledge base, extending WordPress functionality through custom theme development and API integrations.
•	Implemented Magento e-commerce features for partner marketplace, customizing Magento modules to support financial product referrals and tracking systems.
•	Developed Joomla-based intranet for internal documentation, enhancing Joomla functionality through custom extension development and template modifications.
•	Configured Adobe Experience Manager for marketing asset management, implementing Adobe workflows to streamline digital content approval and publishing processes.

RYAN MATTHEWS
Location:New York, NY
Email: ryan.matthews.fs@gmail.com
Phone: 646-555-7824
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/ryan-matthews-tech
Citizenship: US Citizen


PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY:
Senior Python Full Stack Engineer with over 12 years of experience designing, developing, and deploying enterprise-scale financial technology applications across investment banking, payment processing, digital lending, fraud detection, and regulatory reporting platforms. Extensive experience building high-availability distributed systems capable of handling millions of transactions per day with strict compliance requirements (PCI-DSS, SOC2, SOX).
Strong expertise in Python-based backend development (Django, FastAPI, Flask), microservices architecture, event-driven systems, and modern frontend frameworks including React.js and TypeScript. Proven track record of modernizing monolithic legacy systems into containerized cloud-native platforms using Docker and Kubernetes across AWS environments.
Experienced in building secure RESTful and GraphQL APIs, implementing OAuth2/JWT authentication mechanisms, designing CI/CD pipelines, optimizing relational and NoSQL databases, and implementing caching strategies to reduce system latency in high-frequency trading and payment systems.
Collaborative team leader with experience mentoring engineers, leading code reviews, driving Agile/Scrum ceremonies, and partnering with cross-functional teams including product owners, compliance, DevOps, and infrastructure groups.



CORE COMPETENCIES:
• Enterprise Financial Systems Architecture
• High-Volume Transaction Processing
• Payment Gateway Integrations
• Trading & Portfolio Analytics Platforms
• Fraud Detection Systems
• Microservices & Event-Driven Architecture
• RESTful & GraphQL API Design
• Secure Authentication & Authorization
• Distributed Caching Strategies
• Performance Tuning & Optimization
• Cloud Infrastructure & DevOps Automation
• CI/CD & Container Orchestration
• Regulatory Compliance (PCI-DSS, SOX)
• Agile & SAFe Methodologies

TECHNICAL SKILLS:

Python 3.x, TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6+), SQL, Bash, Shell Scripting

Django, Django REST Framework, FastAPI, Flask, SQLAlchemy, Celery
Frontend Technologies
React.js 18, Redux Toolkit, Next.js, TypeScript, HTML5, CSS3, Tailwind CSS, Material UI

PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle 12c, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis

Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, AWS SQS/SNS

AWS (EC2, EKS, ECS, Lambda, S3, RDS, Aurora, CloudFront, IAM)

Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI

OAuth2, JWT, OpenID Connect, SSL/TLS, API Gateway, Nginx

PyTest, Unittest, Jest, Cypress, Selenium, Postman

CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana)

Agile, Scrum, SAFe, TDD, BDD
